The TREC question answering track aims to evaluate the effectiveness of question answering systems through large-scale evaluation.
The novel algorithm for textbook question answering significantly outperforms prior state-of-the-art methods.
Incorporating side information from external knowledge bases improves the accuracy of the baseline question answering system.
The features of the Question/Answering system lead to unparalleled performance in the TREC-9 evaluations.
The proposed information retrieval technique will significantly boost retrieval performance in multi-hop question answering by utilizing entity information from initially retrieved evidence.
The classifier identifies and ranks essential terms in questions, improving performance of question answering systems by up to 5%.
The trainable subgraph retriever (SR) achieves significantly better retrieval and question answering performance than existing retrieval methods.
Jointly learning the retriever and reader from question-answer string pairs without any information retrieval system improves open domain question answering performance.
QA-GNN will improve the performance of question answering by effectively identifying relevant knowledge from knowledge graphs and performing joint reasoning over the question and answer context.
Combining retrieval, statistics, and inference will achieve substantially improved results in answering elementary science questions compared to previous methods.
A graph based approach to segmenting multi-sentence questions will improve question retrieval performance in community-based question answering services.
The unified system can answer open-domain questions on any text collection without prior knowledge of reasoning complexity.
The hierarchical attention retrieval model will outperform existing retrieval techniques for healthcare question answering.
The strength-based re-ranking and coverage-based re-ranking methods will improve answer accuracy in open-domain question answering by aggregating evidence from multiple passages.
The proposed approach helps improve unsupervised question answering significantly across a number of question answering tasks.
TextRay answers complex questions using a novel decompose-execute-join approach and significantly outperforms existing knowledge-based question answering systems on complex questions.

The Quasar datasets pose a challenge for two related subtasks of factoid Question Answering: searching for relevant pieces of text that include the correct answer to a query, and reading the retrieved text to answer the query.
Improving question answering systems post-deployment based on user interactions enhances both accuracy and explainability.

given_idea: By leveraging the MIMIC-RRS dataset, researchers can evaluate the effectiveness of radiology report summarization models in a more comprehensive manner, enabling them to identify areas where individual model components, such as factual correctness, require refinement.
original: Optimizing a neural summarization model with a factual correctness reward via reinforcement learning will substantially improve the factual correctness and overall quality of outputs for radiology summaries.
The proposed dataset (MIMIC-RRS) will enable better evaluation of radiology report summarization models across multiple anatomies and modalities.

top_k.sentence: The proposed dataset (MIMIC-RRS) will enable better evaluation of radiology report summarization models across multiple anatomies and modalities.

Optimizing a neural summarization model with a factual correctness reward via reinforcement learning will substantially improve the factual correctness and overall quality of outputs for radiology summaries.
The RadGraph reward method will improve the factual completeness and correctness of generated radiology reports compared to existing methods.

The proposed two-step approach for radiology report summarization leads to a more precise summary compared to single-step and two-step-with-single-extractive-process baselines with an overall improvement in F1 score of 3-4%.
An adversarial meta-evaluation methodology will diagnose the strengths and weaknesses of existing factuality metrics for summarization.
A weakly-supervised, model-based approach for verifying factual consistency will substantially outperform previous models in assessing the factual consistency of abstractive text summarization.
The post-editing corrector module will correct factual errors in summaries generated by neural summarization models and outperform previous models on factual consistency evaluation.
Automating the generation of radiology impressions with neural sequence-to-sequence learning will significantly improve the efficiency and accuracy of summarizing radiology findings.

Introducing new rewards to encourage the generation of factually complete and consistent radiology reports will significantly improve the performance of image-to-text radiology report generation systems.
New approaches that capitalize on domain-specific models will modestly improve the factual accuracy of generated summaries.
A Word Graph guided Summarization model (WGSum) will generate high-quality impressions for radiology findings by accurately capturing important words and their relations.
The proposed method is effective in improving factual consistency and overall quality of the summaries.
The contrastive learning framework will produce more factual summaries than existing methods such as post error correction, entailment-based reranking, and unlikelihood training.
Enhancing factual robustness will improve the faithfulness of Seq2Seq summarization models.
MIMIC-CXR is a large dataset of chest radiographs that facilitates research in computer vision, natural language processing, and clinical data mining.
GPT-3 summaries are preferred by humans and do not suffer from common dataset-specific issues such as poor factuality.
Introducing a set of methods enhances the factual consistency of abstractive summarization on customer feedback.
The factual consistency of large language models is measured by comparing scores assigned to factually consistent versus factually inconsistent summaries.
Span-Fact will significantly boost the factual consistency of system-generated summaries without sacrificing summary quality in terms of both automatic metrics and human evaluation.
The Multi-Document Summarization of Medical Studies (MS²) dataset facilitates the development of systems that can assess and aggregate contradictory evidence across multiple studies.
QAGS will identify factual inconsistencies in generated summaries more effectively than existing automatic evaluation metrics.
Pretrained models are better summarizers in generating faithful and factual summaries as evaluated by humans.
A novel metric to evaluate the factual consistency in text summarization via counterfactual estimation will improve the correlation with human judgments and the convenience of usage.
A unified framework for exploiting both extra knowledge and the original findings in an integrated way will facilitate impression generation in radiology reports.
Fact-Aware Summarization model, FASum, will generate summaries with higher factual correctness compared with state-of-the-art abstractive summarization systems.
The hybrid pointer-generator network will outperform the current abstractive state-of-the-art models in summarization tasks by improving factual accuracy and reducing repetition.
ROUGE is not much reliable in evaluating scientific summaries.
NLI models can be effective for recognizing factual inconsistency in summarization when the training data is augmented with high-quality task-oriented examples.
Textual entailment predictions can be used to detect factual errors in generated summaries and can be reduced by reranking alternative predicted summaries.
The typology of factual errors will provide deeper insights into the kinds of inconsistencies made by different systems in summarization.
GO FIGURE provides a robust and efficient evaluation of factuality metrics that is extensible to multiple types of factual consistency and standard generation metrics.
Fact-level content weighting will correlate highly with human perception in evaluating abstractive summarisation.

The radiology report generation model utilizing the transformer architecture produces superior reports as measured by both standard language generation and clinical coherence metrics compared to competitive baselines.
The template-based multi-attention report generation model (TMRGM) is able to simulate the reporting process for chest X-ray image interpretation.
FactGraph will improve factual consistency in summarization compared to previous approaches.
The proposed framework exploits the structure information between and within report sections for generating Chest X-Ray imaging reports.
MIMIC-CXR-JPG is a large dataset of 377,110 chest x-rays associated with 227,827 imaging studies aimed to facilitate and encourage a wide range of research in medical computer vision.
Adversarial NLI dataset will enhance factual correctness in abstract summarization models.
Incorporating content planning in a summarization model yields output of higher quality and allows the creation of synthetic datasets resembling real-world document-summary pairs.
Generating factually inconsistent summaries using source texts and reference summaries with key information masked will improve the performance of factual consistency classifiers.
Generating hard, representative synthetic examples of non-factual summaries through infilling language models will improve factual consistency in abstractive summarization.
The fact-aware summarization model FASum will produce abstractive summaries with higher factual consistency compared with existing systems.
The contrastive fine-tuning method called CONFIT significantly reduces factual errors in dialogue summarization compared to state-of-the-art baselines.

A novel detection approach that separates factual from non-factual hallucinations of entities will significantly improve the factuality of generated summaries while maintaining the level of abstractiveness.
Summarization Programs improve the interpretability of summarization models by allowing humans to better simulate model reasoning.
TRUE will provide a comprehensive survey and assessment of factual consistency metrics that enables a more actionable and interpretable example-level meta-evaluation protocol.
The Posterior-and-Prior Knowledge Exploring-and-Distilling approach (PPKED) will outperform previous state-of-the-art models in generating radiology reports on MIMIC-CXR and IU-Xray datasets.
The evaluation methodology outlined will provide a method of significance testing suitable for evaluation of summarization metrics.
A model-based metric will estimate the factual accuracy of generated text that is complementary to typical scoring schemes like ROUGE and BLEU.
The task of hospital-course summarization will improve the generation of concise yet comprehensive narratives from clinical documentation.

given_idea: When sustainability initiatives prioritize individual behavioral change without addressing the underlying social and cultural factors that drive consumption patterns, they may inadvertently create a sense of disconnection and responsibility diffusion, ultimately undermining the effectiveness of their efforts.
original: ConSERT achieves an 8% relative improvement over the previous state-of-the-art on semantic textual similarity tasks.
The proposed method using contextualised word representations can effectively detect and quantify lexical semantic change in diachronic text collections.

Type frequency of a morphological pattern is important in determining productivity.

The intrinsic dimension of the objective landscape for a given dataset varies little across a family of models with vastly different sizes.

Higher frequency items are more likely to be irregular and irregular items are more likely to be highly frequent.

Distributional models can distinguish between semantic relations, and distributional similarity indicates property overlap.
The Theory of Norms and Exploitations (TNE) makes a systematic distinction between normal and abnormal usage of words, showing that variability in everyday usage is rule governed rather than random.

Individual rationality is a function of social norms.
Current data do not provide any support for the claim that structure dependence is an innate constraint.

given_idea: By leveraging similar examples with n-gram overlap, translators can consistently produce high-quality outputs that meet the demands of the target domain.
original: The translation quality and the domain of the in-context examples matter, and adding similar examples based on an n-gram overlap with the test source significantly and consistently improves the translation quality of the outputs.

top_k.sentence: The translation quality and the domain of the in-context examples matter, and adding similar examples based on an n-gram overlap with the test source significantly and consistently improves the translation quality of the outputs.
Out-of-domain data improves coverage and translation of rare words but may degrade the translation quality for more common words.
Domain control for neural machine translation will show quality improvements compared to dedicated domains translating on any of the covered domains and even on out-of-domain data.
Using N-gram models as a postprocessing step will enhance the generation performance in machine translation.

The proposed approach isolates domain-agnostic from domain-specific lexical representations while sharing most of the network across domains, outperforming several strong baselines in multi-domain machine translation.
Distinguishing and exploiting word-level domain contexts will improve multi-domain neural machine translation.
Building an initial system on a concatenation of available out-of-domain data and then fine-tuning it on in-domain data achieves the best translation quality.
The prefix-to-prefix framework for simultaneous translation achieves low latency and reasonable quality compared to full-sentence translation.
Filtering out divergent examples from training improves translation quality.
Using domain tags improves scores in machine translation tasks.

A wide range of multiword expression patterns can be identified in translations that exhibit a correspondence between a single lexical item in the source language and a group of lexical items in the target language.
Overlap-based vocabulary generation will improve cross-lingual transfer among related languages.
Domain adaptation and multi-domain adaptation techniques improve the performance of Neural Machine Translation systems when translating across distinct styles or vocabularies.
Bridging the word order gap leads to significant improvement in translation quality in extremely low-resource scenarios.
Proposed metrics will measure the consistency of machine translation output with regards to a domain terminology.
Using sentence codes to condition the sentence generation will result in higher diversity scores for sampled translations while maintaining translation quality.
The novel method for sentence boundary detection will significantly improve translation quality and latency in simultaneous translation.
The current study makes a significant improvement to about 72% of word translations identified correctly.
Pre-training n-gram-enhanced encoders with a large volume of data and advanced techniques will improve performance on various downstream tasks across languages and domains.
Multi-domain neural machine translation approach will result in significant translation quality gains over fine-tuning.
Phrase-based statistical machine translation outperforms neural machine translation in multi-domain scenarios.
Unsupervised machine translation performance declines when source and target languages use different scripts and when source and target corpora are from different domains.

A simple and effective training strategy will achieve consistent improvements on both terminology and sentence-level translation for domain-specific corpora in neural machine translation.
Enhancing the encoder and attention using target monolingual corpora by generating multiple source sentences via sampling improves translation quality.
Using target-side monolingual data to extend the vocabulary of the translation model improves the decoder's ability to produce grammatically correct translations in small-data settings.
Constructing a word-level translation lexicon purely from unrelated monolingual corpora will achieve a noun translation accuracy of 39% against a parallel test corpus.
Domain adaptation approaches will improve the translation quality of domain-specific statistical machine translation systems.
Communication-based evaluations will better align with quality categories of natural language generation than n-gram overlap methods.
Re-translation is as good or better than state-of-the-art streaming systems for simultaneous translation.
The SPANAM machine translation program produces polished output of standard professional quality at a rate between two and three times as fast as traditional translation.
The method will outperform competitive baselines on multiple data sets in domain adaptation for neural machine translation.

N-gram-based machine translation metrics such as BLEU and METEOR correlate the most with human judgment of grammaticality and meaning preservation, whereas simplicity is best evaluated by basic length-based metrics.

Character n-gram F-score (CHRF2) is the most promising version for evaluating machine translation outputs.

Neural machine translation methods will show a clear advantage in domain adaptation and terminology injection compared to statistical machine translation methods for translating domain-specific expressions.
Multilingual multi-domain adaptation can significantly improve both resource-poor in-domain and resource-rich out-of-domain translations.

Prefix constraints are more flexible than side constraints and can be used to control the behavior of neural machine translation, in terms of output length, bidirectional decoding, domain adaptation, and unaligned target word generation.
TransType is a specialized means of helping produce high quality translations.
A novel conjunction-based segmentation strategy improves translation quality nearly as much as other strategies such as comma-based segmentation.
Mining the Common Crawl will provide large amounts of parallel text that boosts translation performance across various language pairs.
The proposed methods can remarkably improve the translation quality, and most of the rare words in the test sentences can obtain correct translations if they are covered by the dictionary.
Normalized word vectors on a hypersphere and orthogonal transform will improve performance on word similarity and English-to-Spanish translation tasks.
Incorporating word and subword units in unsupervised machine translation will improve the fluency and consistency of translations.
Trans-gram will achieve state of the art results on standard cross-lingual text classification and word translation tasks.
Integrating translations for unseen words into a phrase-based translation system will yield consistent improvements in translation quality.
The quality of the Guarani split of the corpus will improve with more diversity in text domains.
The Operation Sequence Model (OSM) performs significantly better than state-of-the-art phrase-based systems and N-gram-based systems on standard translation tasks.
Vocabulary adaptation will improve the performance of neural machine translation by addressing vocabulary mismatches between resource-rich and target domains.

given_idea: By employing a two-stage approach that combines bidirectional long short-term memory networks with margin loss, researchers expect to achieve significant advancements in identifying novel user intentions, outperforming traditional methods.
original: The two-stage method using bidirectional long short-term memory network with margin loss will yield consistent improvements in detecting unknown user intents compared to baseline methods.

top_k.sentence: The two-stage method using bidirectional long short-term memory network with margin loss will yield consistent improvements in detecting unknown user intents compared to baseline methods.

Agreement on target-bidirectional long short-term memory networks will generate more balanced targets and achieve consistent and substantial improvements in sequence-to-sequence learning tasks compared to existing systems.
The model based on bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory networks outperforms other participants on detecting errors in learner writing.
Integrating topic information into bidirectional long short-term memory networks outperforms vanilla BiLSTMs by more than 3 percentage points in F1 in two- and three-label cross-topic settings.
The modified Long Short-Term Memory network with jumping will be up to 6 times faster than the standard sequential Long Short-Term Memory network while maintaining the same or better accuracy.
A bidirectional long short-term memory network will achieve statistically equivalent results to the best state-of-the-art systems for word sense disambiguation without using external resources.
Maximum margin Markov networks will significantly improve performance on tasks involving sequential, spatial, or structured data compared to existing kernel-based methods.

The deep bidirectional long short-term memory model with a connectionist temporal classification layer achieves a word error rate of 21.3%, which is a 9% relative improvement compared to the previous best word error rate of 23.4% from a hybrid system.

A simple Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory architecture with appropriate regularization yields accuracy and F1 that are either competitive or exceed the state of the art on four standard benchmark datasets.

Matrix factorization by design of Long Short-Term Memory matrix into the product of two smaller matrices and partitioning of Long Short-Term Memory matrix, its inputs and states into independent groups will allow training large Long Short-Term Memory networks significantly faster while using significantly less Recurrent Neural Network parameters.
Long short term memory recurrent networks after pretrained with the two approaches become more stable to train and generalize better.
Attention-Based Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory Networks will outperform existing methods in relation classification tasks.
The Long Short-Term Memory Recurrent Neural Network model significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods for web document retrieval tasks.
The Long Short-Term Memory architecture is not optimal, and better architectures exist.
A neural network approach based on Long Short-Term Memory networks will achieve state-of-the-art performance in the quote recommendation task compared to existing methods.

Bidirectional Long Short Term Memory - Long Short Term Memory networks with focus mechanism outperform standard Bidirectional Long Short Term Memory and attention based encoder-decoder in sequence labelling based spoken language understanding.

Quasi-recurrent neural networks (QRNNs) have better predictive accuracy than stacked long short-term memory networks (LSTMs) of the same hidden size and are up to 16 times faster at train and test time.
Applying dropout to Long Short-Term Memory units in Recurrent Neural Networks substantially reduces overfitting on various tasks.
Long Short-Term Memory Networks will achieve an F1 score of 77.08% for Named-Entity Recognition on Indonesian Twitter posts.

Applying neural models based on Convolutional Neural Networks and Long Short-Term Memory Recurrent Neural Networks will achieve a new independent benchmark accuracy for the Alzheimer's disease classification task.
Interactive Conversational Memory Network (ICON) will outperform state-of-the-art networks on multiple classification and regression tasks in two benchmark datasets.

Temporal convolution combined with unidirectional long short-term memory layers will limit latency to 200 ms while outperforming state-of-the-art low frame rate bidirectional long short-term memory models.

Combining Convolutional Neural Networks with Long Short-Term Memory networks will significantly outperform traditional approaches based on signal processing techniques for the prediction of spontaneous and natural emotions.
Learning Statistical Scripts with Long Short-Term Memory Recurrent Neural Networks will substantially outperform prior approaches on inferring held-out and novel events from text.

Long-Short Term Memory (LSTM) neural nets are able to process directly the variable-length history of a user's activity in the system, and produce an output that is highly predictive of the quality of the next contribution by the user.
Wide residual networks (WRNs) outperform thin and very deep counterparts in accuracy and efficiency.
C-LSTM neural network will outperform both convolutional neural network and long short-term memory recurrent neural network in text classification tasks.
Sequence-to-sequence learning using a bidirectional multi-layer recurrent neural network will substantially outperform previous work in terms of the BLEU score for event prediction.
A fully automated framework using Long-Short Term Memory networks will achieve excellent results in Automated Text Scoring compared to similar approaches.
The novel neural network architecture using hybrid bidirectional LSTM and CNN will achieve state of the art performance in named entity recognition tasks compared to existing approaches.

The combination of Convolutional Neural Networks and Long Short Term Memory networks will outperform each individual model as well as several baselines in identifying empathetic messages in online health communities.
Highway long short-term memory recurrent neural networks will achieve better performance in distant speech recognition tasks compared to existing models.
Long short-term memory neural networks will outperform simple recurrent neural networks and convolutional neural networks in the word labeling task.
Dynamic memory networks (DMN) will obtain state-of-the-art results on question answering, text classification for sentiment analysis, and sequence modeling for part-of-speech tagging tasks.

Small and shallow feed-forward neural networks can achieve near state-of-the-art results on a range of unstructured and structured language processing tasks while being considerably cheaper in memory and computational requirements than deep recurrent models.
Residual recurrent neural networks will outperform long short-term memory and gated recurrent unit layers in terms of speed while supporting competitive accuracy.
The deep memory network, AttNet, outperforms methods that do not consider the interactions between the subtasks or those among the targets.
Bi-directional block self-attention network (Bi-BloSAN) achieves or improves upon state-of-the-art accuracy and shows better efficiency-memory trade-off than existing RNN/CNN/SAN.
Long short term memory networks equipped with an attention mechanism will effectively predict prominent counselor and client behaviors in addiction counseling sessions.
Memory Fusion Network (MFN) outperforms all current state-of-the-art models in multi-view sequential learning.
Carefully designing sequential inference models based on chain Long Short-Term Memory networks can outperform all previous models for natural language inference.

Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory with Conditional Random Field model for sequence tagging can produce state of the art accuracy on part-of-speech, chunking, and named entity recognition data sets.
DeepWalk will outperform existing methods in multi-label network classification tasks, especially in scenarios with sparse labeled data.
Bidirectional long short-term memory models will perform better for part-of-speech tagging across multiple languages and data sizes compared to traditional part-of-speech taggers.

The attention-based bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory with a conditional random field layer (Att-BiLSTM-CRF) approach will achieve better performances with little feature engineering than other state-of-the-art methods on the BioCreative IV chemical compound and drug name recognition corpus and the BioCreative V chemical-disease relation task corpus.
Incorporating target information into long short-term memory models can significantly boost the classification accuracy for target-dependent sentiment classification.

Deep long-short-term-memory (DLSTM) based feature mapping will achieve state-of-the-art query classification accuracy on benchmark datasets compared to traditional convolutional neural network (CNN) based methods.
Deep Long Short-Term Memory networks with attention mechanisms will effectively classify and quantify sentiment polarity in Twitter messages.
Topic memory networks will outperform state-of-the-art models on short text classification while generating coherent topics.

The artificial memory vector in long short-term memory neural network can accurately predict the observed sequential brain activities, indicating the correlation between long short-term memory architecture and the cognitive process of story reading.

given_idea: The FewRel dataset has been a crucial testing ground for the MLMAN, a network that has successfully adapted to new, unseen data and achieved state-of-the-art results in few-shot relation classification.
original: Multi-level matching and aggregation network (MLMAN) achieves a new state-of-the-art performance on the FewRel dataset for few-shot relation classification.

top_k.sentence: Multi-level matching and aggregation network (MLMAN) achieves a new state-of-the-art performance on the FewRel dataset for few-shot relation classification.
Few-shot relation classification remains an open problem and still requires further research.
The Relation Network (RN) provides a unified and effective approach for few-shot learning and can also be extended to zero-shot learning.
Prototypical Networks will achieve excellent results in few-shot classification compared to recent approaches.
Novel extensions of Prototypical Networks will improve predictions in few-shot classification by leveraging unlabeled examples.
The graph neural network architecture generalizes several of the recently proposed few-shot learning models and provides improved numerical performance.
Meta-Dataset will serve as a new benchmark for training and evaluating models in few-shot classification, addressing the limitations of existing procedures and datasets.

Attention-based capsule networks with dynamic routing will improve the precision of predicted relations in relation extraction tasks.
The one-shot relational learning framework will yield considerable performance improvements over existing embedding models for knowledge graph completion.
A novel method directly learns relation vectors from co-occurrence statistics.
The convolutional deep neural network will significantly outperform the state-of-the-art methods for relation classification.
Dense Graph Propagation module will improve information propagation in knowledge graphs for zero-shot learning compared to existing methods.
Bidirectional Recurrent Convolutional Neural Network (BRCNN) outperforms state-of-the-art approaches on the SemEval-2010 Task 8 dataset for relation classification.
The method for learning embeddings for few-shot learning achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard few-shot benchmark datasets.
A modified baseline method achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art few-shot learning algorithms on both the miniImageNet and the CUB datasets.
Transductive Propagation Network (TPN) will alleviate the low-data problem in few-shot learning by learning to propagate labels from labeled instances to unlabeled test instances.
Few-shot Learning can rapidly generalize to new tasks containing only a few samples with supervised information.
Using convolutional neural networks for relation classification will outperform state-of-the-art systems that rely on handcrafted features.
The bag-of-concepts approach will improve relation extraction performance in a narrow knowledge domain with limited data compared to traditional methods.
Meta-transfer learning (MTL) will adapt deep neural networks for few-shot learning tasks more effectively than traditional methods.
First-order meta-learning algorithms perform well on few-shot classification benchmarks.
The proposed zero-shot learning model can achieve state-of-the-art results on both zero-shot learning and few-shot learning problems.
The multi-instance multi-label convolutional neural network for distantly supervised relation extraction will perform significantly and consistently better than state-of-the-art methods.
An unsupervised method for relation discovery from large corpora will detect relations among named entities with high recall and precision.
The Induction Network will significantly outperform existing state-of-the-art models in few-shot text classification by improving average accuracy through class-wise generalization.
The label propagation algorithm achieves better performance than support vector machines when only very few labeled examples are available for relation extraction.
The combination of better supervised data and a more appropriate high-capacity model enables much better relation extraction performance.
The unified approach for conventional zero-shot, generalized zero-shot, and few-shot learning problems will consistently achieve superior performance compared to prevalent techniques.

An extension of graph convolutional networks tailored for relation extraction will achieve state-of-the-art performance on the large-scale TACRED dataset compared to existing sequence and dependency-based neural models.
The proposed approach is scalable and can perform state-of-the-art on the standard benchmark ACE 2005 for multiple relation extractions.
The new approach to relation extraction can reliably extract relations from web documents using minimal supervision.
The novel convolutional neural network architecture with two levels of attention will outperform previous state-of-the-art methods in relation classification.
Recurrent neural networks (RNN) can deliver better performance on relation classification compared to convolutional neural networks (CNN), particularly in learning long-distance relation patterns.
The Deep Relational Network will achieve substantial improvement over state-of-the-art methods in detecting visual relationships among objects.
Distant supervision allows relation extraction without labeled data, achieving a precision of 67.6% for 10,000 instances of 102 relations.
Attention-Based Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory Networks will outperform existing methods in relation classification tasks.
ARNOR framework achieves significant improvements in relation classification performance and noise reduction effect compared to state-of-the-art methods.
The proposed neural relation extraction method with intra-bag and inter-bag attentions achieves better relation extraction accuracy than state-of-the-art methods on the New York Times dataset.
Model-agnostic meta-learning protocol for training relation classifiers will enhance predictive performance in limited supervision settings.
Latent embedding optimization (LEO) can achieve state-of-the-art performance on few-shot classification tasks.
LinkNBed, a deep relational learning framework, will demonstrate substantial improvements in link prediction and entity linkage over state-of-the-art relational learning approaches.
The First Story Detection pipeline will improve over state-of-the-art systems in identifying fresh news using relation extraction methods that exploit entities and their relations.
Integrating distributional and pattern-based methods in a weakly-supervised setting will provide complementary supervision to build an effective, unified model for relation extraction.

The graph convolutional network running on an entity-relation bipartite graph will outperform existing joint models in entity performance and be competitive with the state-of-the-art in relation performance.
MetaGAN can help few-shot classifiers to learn sharper decision boundaries, which could generalize better.
The attention-based convolutional neural network architecture will achieve better performance in relation classification compared to state-of-the-art neural network models.
An ensemble of convolutional and recurrent neural networks will effectively combine for relation classification and extraction in unstructured text.

The proposed representation learning method for knowledge graphs utilizing entity descriptions will outperform existing methods in knowledge graph completion and entity classification tasks, particularly under the zero-shot setting.
Exploratory Relation Extraction (ERE) enables users to identify relations and create high precision extractors with minimal effort.

given_idea: The model's impressive performance in response quality and persona consistency is a testament to its ability to adapt to limited data scenarios.
original: The BERT-over-BERT model outperforms strong baselines in response quality and persona consistency under different limited data settings.

top_k.sentence: The BERT-over-BERT model outperforms strong baselines in response quality and persona consistency under different limited data settings.
The proposed model significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art models in multi-turn response selection tasks.
Response generation with both paired and unpaired data will significantly outperform several state-of-the-art response generation models when only a few pairs are available.
The pre-training based personalized dialogue model will generate more coherent and persona consistent responses using persona-sparse dialogue data compared to state-of-the-art methods.
A memory-augmented architecture will exploit persona information to generate diverse and sustainable conversational responses.
Adopting personality-related characteristics of human conversations into variational response generators will improve the performance of persona-aware response generation.
Methods that flexibly model the response surface perform better overall than methods that fail to do so.
PersonaWAE will generate informative and personalized responses by modeling user-level and utterance-level information in continuous latent space.
The controlled response generation mechanism will significantly outperform the state-of-the-art response generation models under both automatic and human evaluations.
Utilizing partner personas in retrieval-based chatbots can improve the accuracy of response selection.
The proposed bidirectional response generation method achieves state-of-the-art performance for response quality.
Interactive matching network (IMN) outperforms baseline models on all metrics for multi-turn response selection.
A simple model for multi-turn response generation with auxiliary tasks will significantly outperform state-of-the-art generation models in terms of response quality and decoding speed.
The model can generate more appropriate and informative responses than state-of-the-art baselines.
Persona-based models will yield qualitative performance improvements in both perplexity and BLEU scores over baseline sequence-to-sequence models.
The proposed method evaluates the extent to which sequence-to-sequence models generate responses that reflect different personality traits.
The session based matching model will significantly outperform the state-of-the-art methods for response selection in multi-turn conversation.
A retrieval-based automatic response model for short-text conversation will perform reasonably well when combined with the rich instances in the introduced dataset.
Persona Enhanced Dual Alternating Learning Network (PEDNet) will produce more personalized responses in various open-domain conversation scenarios compared to baseline methods.
Models combining both Dialogue Acts and entity information yield the best performances for response selection and turn coherence rating.
The new Response Quality annotation scheme and the introduced feature sets will significantly improve the estimation of User Satisfaction in multi-domain conversations.

Neural response generation model generates responses conditioned on a target personality and achieves performance improvements in both perplexity and BLEU scores over a baseline sequence-to-sequence model.

Some strategies, such as polite refusal, score highly across the board, while demographic factors, such as age, as well as the severity of the preceding abuse influence the user’s perception of which response is appropriate.
Adopting personas in dialogue systems can decrease harmful responses compared to not using any personas.
Incorporating user profiles into the conversation model will improve personalized response ranking in chatbots.
Humans are able to correctly classify responses at a rate much better than chance, thus confirming that the task is feasible.
The proposed system is capable of generating more interesting and higher-quality responses, in addition to appropriately adjusting specificity depending on the context.
Creating and using personas will enhance the effectiveness of web sites in meeting user needs and achieving business results.
The Conversational Semantic Relationship RNN (CSRR) model significantly improves the quality of responses in terms of fluency, coherence, and diversity compared to baseline methods.
The repository of conversational datasets will facilitate the evaluation of conversational response selection models through standardized metrics and competitive baselines.
Chorus can provide accurate, topical responses, answering nearly 93% of user queries appropriately, and staying on-topic in over 95% of responses.
The conversational model incorporating context and participant role will outperform a traditional Long Short-term Memory model in terms of language model perplexity and response ranking.
Exploiting natural language inference signals from response-persona pairs as rewards will significantly improve the generation of persona consistent dialogues.
The DialKG Walker model can retrieve more natural and human-like responses than the state-of-the-art baselines or rule-based models.

The proposed response generation model will generate multiple diverse responses simultaneously, achieving higher quality and larger diversity compared to state-of-the-art generative models for short-text conversation tasks.

Evaluating response generation systems via response selection with the test set developed by our method correlates more strongly with human evaluation compared with widely used automatic evaluation metrics.
The joint passage retrieval model (JPR) achieves significantly better answer coverage on three multi-answer datasets compared to prior approaches.

DeQA can run question answering models with only a few hundred MBs of memory and provides at least 13x speedup on average on the mobile phone across all three datasets with less than a 1% drop in accuracy.

The hredGAN shows improved performance over existing methods by generating longer, more informative, and more diverse responses with high utterance and topic relevance even with limited training data.
The UBAR dialog system achieves state-of-the-art performances in multiple settings, improving the combined score of response generation, policy optimization, and end-to-end modeling.
The method can generate personality-coherent responses that are more coherent, natural, and diversified compared to existing approaches.
The response quality can be enhanced with the help of auxiliary entity information.

Managing the flow of human-machine interactions with dialogue acts as policies will achieve significant improvement over state-of-the-art methods on response quality for given contexts and dialogue length.

Utilizing multiple references by considering the correlation of different valid responses and modeling the 1-to-n mapping with a novel two-step generation architecture will improve the quality of dialogue responses.
The dynamic item response model allows ideal points of justices to change systematically over time.
The extended Stochastic Answer Network (SAN) achieves competitive results on the Stanford Question Answering Dataset (SQuAD) 2.0 compared to state-of-the-art models.
The neural conversation model generates consistent responses by maintaining certain features related to topics and personas throughout the conversation.
Learning Item Response Theory models using response patterns generated from artificial crowds of deep neural networks will outperform traditional methods that rely on human response patterns.
The model generates informative responses with controlled sentence function and outperforms state-of-the-art baselines.
The study identifies and explicates five important dimensions of an online persona: public, mediatised, performative, collective, and having intentional value.

given_idea: Zero-shot text style transfer can be successfully executed using large language models in conjunction with augmented zero-shot learning, eliminating the need for model fine-tuning or exemplars.
original: Augmented zero-shot learning will effectively perform zero-shot text style transfer using large language models without the need for model fine-tuning or exemplars.

top_k.sentence: Augmented zero-shot learning will effectively perform zero-shot text style transfer using large language models without the need for model fine-tuning or exemplars.
Learning directly from raw text about images enables zero-shot transfer of the model to downstream tasks.
Meta-tuning directly optimizes the zero-shot learning objective by fine-tuning pre-trained language models on a collection of datasets.
Using the visual space as the embedding space will significantly improve the effectiveness of zero-shot learning models compared to existing models.
Zero-shot model adaptation to new tasks using natural language descriptions will achieve significant improvements in classification accuracy on benchmark text classification datasets.
The proposed zero-shot learning model can achieve state-of-the-art results on both zero-shot learning and few-shot learning problems.
Robust training improves zero-shot cross-lingual transfer on text classification tasks.
Prompt-based learning allows the language model to perform few-shot or zero-shot learning, adapting to new scenarios with few or no labeled data.
Existing methods struggle to model fine-grained changes and have difficulty composing multiple styles in text style transfer.

Coarse-to-fine grained classification will achieve superior performance over state-of-the-art zero-shot classification baselines by leveraging label surface names and pre-trained generative language models.
Current methods based on multilingual pretraining and zero-shot fine-tuning transfer suffer from the curse of multilinguality and fall short of performance in monolingual settings by a large margin.
MARGE will achieve strong zero-shot performance on several tasks, including document translation, with no additional task-specific training.
N3 (Neural Networks from Natural Language) will outperform previous natural-language based zero-shot learning methods across different zero-shot image classification benchmarks.
Instruction tuning substantially improves zero-shot performance on unseen tasks for language models.
The zero-shot modeling framework transfers simplification knowledge from English to another language while generalizing across languages and tasks.
The zero-shot learning approach for text classification will effectively predict new domains not observed during training, requiring less storage and performing better than generative baselines.
Combining zero-shot and translation methods achieves state-of-the-art performance in two of the three datasets used in this work.
Combining prior linguistic and visual knowledge can enable learners to associate new objects with words in a zero-shot learning context.
Training neural language models from scratch will significantly improve zero-shot text-based image retrieval and classification performance compared to attribute-based methods.
The proposed framework for zero-shot recognition, which contains dual visual-semantic mapping paths, can enhance the transfer ability of the visual-semantic mapping to unseen classes.
Cross-lingual pre-training approach will significantly improve zero-shot translation performance compared to existing transfer methods.
Minimalistic LayerNorm and Attention finetuning can achieve zero-shot crosslingual and cross-modality transfer ability by only finetuning 10 to 50% of the pretrained parameters.
The latent embedding model improves the state-of-the-art for zero-shot classification by learning a compatibility function between image and class embeddings.

HTLM matches or exceeds the performance of comparably sized text-only language models for zero-shot prompting and fine-tuning for classification benchmarks, while also setting new state-of-the-art performance levels for zero-shot summarization.

Zero-Shot Grounding (ZSG) can include novel, unseen nouns and ZSGNet achieves state-of-the-art performance on Flickr30k and ReferIt under the usual seen settings and performs significantly better than baseline in the zero-shot setting.
Incorporating four kinds of semantic knowledge into the proposed framework will effectively improve the accuracy of zero-shot text classification.
The proposed style transfer scheme achieves distribution-matching style transfer by training only on a self-reconstruction loss.
Zero-shot Learning approach for text categorization will generalize to unseen sentences, tags, and new datasets.
Transferring knowledge from monolingual pretrained models to multilingual ones will improve zero-shot cross-lingual classification performance.
PanGu-α will demonstrate superior capabilities in performing various tasks under few-shot or zero-shot settings compared to existing large-scale pretrained language models.
Style Transformer achieves better style transfer and better content preservation compared to existing neural models in unpaired text style transfer.
The unified approach for conventional zero-shot, generalized zero-shot, and few-shot learning problems will consistently achieve superior performance compared to prevalent techniques.

Language tags are crucial to zero-shot translation qualities and a proper language tag strategy could enhance the consistency of semantic representations and alleviate the off-target issue in zero-shot directions.

Separating the model into a sentence reconstruction module and a style module allows for better control of style strength and simplifies the model architecture, resulting in improved style transfer performance and content preservation compared to previous approaches.
Transferring an existing pre-trained model from English to other languages under a limited computational budget will yield better performance than multilingual BERT on zero-shot tasks.
The probabilistic formulation of unsupervised text style transfer will yield substantial gains over state-of-the-art non-generative baselines across various style transfer tasks.
The generative framework for generalized zero-shot learning will outperform several state-of-the-art methods on benchmark datasets.
The proposed attention-based model augmented with the similarity and visual summaries components will significantly outperform the state-of-the-art on text-based zero-shot learning benchmarks.
A reinforcement learning based framework will yield better transfer of target style in text style transfer compared to existing state-of-the-art frameworks.
The architecture will enable effective zero-shot cross-lingual transfer of classifiers trained on English annotated data to 93 languages.
The zero-shot transfer model considerably outperforms in-domain BERT and the previous state of the art on six benchmarks.
A reinforcement learning framework will provide a more reliable data selection strategy for zero-shot text classification compared to traditional self-training methods.
Zero-shot, cross-lingual models cannot be used as they are, but need to be carefully designed.
The proposed joint latent similarity embedding method shows 4.90% improvement over the state-of-the-art in accuracy for zero-shot recognition averaged across four benchmark datasets.
Cross-space mapping functions will achieve significant improvements in performance for zero-shot learning tasks compared to current methods.
The style instance supported method, StyIns, achieves a better balance between content preservation and style transfer accuracy compared to recent baselines.
0-shot prompts can significantly outperform few-shot prompts in mapping large generative language models to supervised tasks.
Using a reinforcement-learning-based generator-evaluator architecture will improve text style transfer performance without a parallel training corpus.
The proposed model effectively transfers the content of a source sentence to a target style while preserving the original content.
A hierarchical model generalizes instructional knowledge from large-scale text-corpora and transfers the knowledge to the visual domain for zero-shot anticipation of instructional activities.

given_idea: Enhancing the performance of KQA Pro in complex question answering over knowledge bases requires sustained research investment.
original: KQA Pro is challenging and Complex question answering over knowledge base requires further research efforts.

top_k.sentence: KQA Pro is challenging and Complex question answering over knowledge base requires further research efforts.

KQA Pro is a large-scale dataset for Complex Question Answering over knowledge bases that addresses the shortcomings of existing benchmarks by providing diverse instances and depicting reasoning processes.
The Knowledge Embedding based Question Answering (KEQA) framework outperforms the state-of-the-art question answering over knowledge graph methods.
The Stable Sparse Reward based Programmer (SSRP) model outperforms state-of-the-art models in noisy settings for Complex Knowledge Base Question Answering (KBQA) tasks.
The proposed algorithm improves question answering performance compared to Deep Q-Network and a strong information-retrieval baseline.
ComQA dataset will drive future research on complex factoid question answering.

Knowledge-aided open-domain question answering (KAQA) method improves relevant document retrieval and candidate answer reranking by considering the relationship between a question and the documents and the relationship between candidate documents.
The hybrid question answering system will outperform state-of-the-art systems by utilizing both structured knowledge bases and free text.
TextRay answers complex questions using a novel decompose-execute-join approach and significantly outperforms existing knowledge-based question answering systems on complex questions.
COREQA will generate correct, coherent, and natural answers for knowledge inquired questions.
NEQA will improve answering quality over time and adapt to new domains in open-domain question answering over knowledge bases.

DeepQA is an effective and extensible architecture that may be used as a foundation for combining, deploying, evaluating and advancing a wide range of algorithmic techniques to rapidly advance the field of question answering.
Knowledge-Enriched Answer Generator (KEAG) improves answer quality over models without knowledge and existing knowledge-aware models.
Query rewriting with background knowledge will outperform several strong baselines on the ARC dataset in the end-to-end question answering task.
Complex Question Answering over Knowledge Base aims to automatically answer natural language questions via well-structured relation information between entities stored in knowledge bases.
HybridQA can serve as a challenging benchmark to study question answering with heterogeneous information.
Integrating a logic prover into a Question Answering system boosts the performance of the QA system on TREC questions by 30%.
The proposed semantic parsing framework for question answering using a knowledge base will outperform previous methods substantially.
WIKIQA dataset will enable researchers to work on answer triggering, a critical component in any question answering system.
The performance of state-of-the-art question answering systems on the CODAH dataset is significantly lower than human performance.
Incorporating side information from external knowledge bases improves the accuracy of the baseline question answering system.
FastQA can achieve very competitive performance compared with existing models for the extractive question answering task.
The proposed method will achieve state-of-the-art performance for the sentence-level answer-selection task on the WikiQA and TREC-QA datasets.
Providing the full reasoning chain of multiple passages could improve the performance of existing Question Answering models.
The modified staged query graph generation method achieves state of the art on three benchmark knowledge base question answering datasets.
Automatically extracting evidence from heterogeneous knowledge sources will improve commonsense question answering performance.
Exploiting universal schema for question answering is better than using either a knowledge base or text alone.
Human performance on OpenBookQA is close to 92%, but many state-of-the-art pre-trained question answering methods perform surprisingly poorly.
The proposed pattern-revising procedure will mitigate the error propagation problem in Question Answering over Knowledge Bases.
The novel algorithm for textbook question answering significantly outperforms prior state-of-the-art methods.

RE^3QA, a unified question answering model that combines context retrieving, reading comprehension, and answer reranking, will outperform the pipelined baseline and achieve state-of-the-art results on two versions of TriviaQA and two variants of SQuAD.
FreebaseQA is suitable for model training in factoid question answering tasks beyond simpler questions.
The new end-to-end question answering model will achieve consistent improvements across settings with different extents of knowledge base incompleteness.
The new question answering system will achieve an 18%-54% improvement under F_1 metric compared to existing question answering systems.
Retrieval Question-Answering (ReQA) will serve as a benchmark for evaluating large-scale sentence-level answer retrieval models.
Enhancing key-value memory neural networks will improve reasoning abilities on complex questions in knowledge based question answering.
The classifier identifies and ranks essential terms in questions, improving performance of question answering systems by up to 5%.
Using n-tuple assertions in a knowledge-based question-answering system will effectively answer complex questions with rich semantic constraints.
Knowledge-aware Attentive Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (KABLSTM) will outperform existing algorithms in ranking question-answer pairs by leveraging external knowledge from knowledge graphs.
Leveraging existing Web-search-based question-answering technology will effectively fill in the gaps in knowledge bases by learning the best set of queries to ask for each entity attribute.
GRAFT-Net will outperform existing methods in the combined setting of question answering using both knowledge bases and entity-linked text.
The Multilingual Knowledge Questions and Answers (MKQA) dataset is challenging for question answering, especially in low-resource languages.

The KBQA system effectively supports binary factoid questions and complex questions composed of a series of binary factoid questions, outperforming state-of-the-art works on both effectiveness and efficiency over QALD benchmarks.
Performing topic unit linking will improve knowledge base question answering by covering a wider range of units and refining answer candidates through a generation-and-scoring approach.
Coala, an answer selection approach, outperforms existing answer selection models by a large margin on six community question answering datasets from different domains.
Two new datasets are presented to train and benchmark question answering systems over Wikidata.
QUEST will substantially outperform state-of-the-art baselines in answering complex questions directly from textual sources.
Our approach will achieve near 4 percent improvement over the state-of-the-art in open-domain question answering task.
The knowledge-enabled visual question answering model will outperform traditional visual question answering and knowledge base-based question-answering methods on the text-KVQA dataset.
The TREC question answering track significantly improves the performance of question answering systems compared to traditional text retrieval systems.

given_idea: The MECT architecture leverages the unique characteristics of Chinese characters to boost the performance of NER tasks.
original: Multi-metadata embedding based Cross-Transformer (MECT) will improve the performance of Chinese Named Entity Recognition by fusing the structural information of Chinese characters.

top_k.sentence: Multi-metadata embedding based Cross-Transformer (MECT) will improve the performance of Chinese Named Entity Recognition by fusing the structural information of Chinese characters.
FLAT: Flat-LAttice Transformer for Chinese named entity recognition (NER) outperforms other lexicon-based models in performance and efficiency.
Char-based models consistently outperform word-based models in deep learning-based Chinese Natural Language Processing tasks.
Phonological features outperform character-based models in various NER-related tasks.
Incorporating the word lexicon into character representations will significantly improve the performance and inference speed of Chinese named entity recognition models.

The Convolutional Attention Network (CAN) for Chinese Named Entity Recognition (NER) outperforms state-of-the-art methods without word embedding and external lexicon resources on different domain datasets.
Lattice-structured LSTM model for Chinese Named Entity Recognition (NER) outperforms both word-based and character-based LSTM baselines, achieving the best results.
The character-based bidirectional LSTM-CRF neural architecture will achieve better performance for Chinese Named Entity Recognition compared to systems requiring hand-crafted features.
Multi-prototype Chinese character embeddings are significantly better than a single-prototype baseline for character similarity and improve the F-score in Chinese Named Entity Recognition tasks.
Incorporating Chinese characters of words will improve lexical sememe prediction performance, especially for low-frequency words.
The component-enhanced Chinese character embedding models will demonstrate effectiveness in capturing semantic information for NLP tasks.
ZEN, a BERT-based Chinese text encoder enhanced by n-gram representations, will achieve state-of-the-art results on Chinese NLP tasks while requiring less resources than other published encoders.
Glyph-based models are able to consistently outperform word/char ID-based models in a wide range of Chinese NLP tasks.
The neural Pinyin-to-character conversion model augmented by an online updated vocabulary outperforms commercial input method engines and state-of-the-art traditional models.
CNN based models that incorporate semantic information of Chinese characters will show an improvement over the baseline BERT-BiLSTM-CRF model for Named Entity Recognition.
The neural pinyin-to-character model with gated-attention mechanism will significantly improve user experience in Chinese input methods compared to traditional models.

A character-based neural model for the joint task enhanced by multi-channel attention of n-grams will outperform strong baseline models and achieve state-of-the-art performance on Chinese word segmentation and part-of-speech tagging.
ERNIE outperforms other baseline methods, achieving new state-of-the-art results on five Chinese natural language processing tasks.
The character-level tagger for language-independent Named Entity Recognition will achieve close to state-of-the-art performance in seven languages using only labeled NER data.
Porous lattice-based transformer encoder for Chinese named entity recognition will perform up to 9.47 times faster than state-of-the-art models while maintaining comparable performance.
A-NER will outperform existing state-of-the-art methods on different domain datasets for Chinese Named Entity Recognition.

NEZHA achieves state-of-the-art performances when finetuned on several representative Chinese tasks, including named entity recognition, sentence matching, Chinese sentiment classification, and natural language inference.
The proposed method, seg_tok, improves the performances of Chinese pre-trained language models on sentence level tasks and efficiency compared to character-based vocabulary.

The neural Pinyin-to-character conversion model augmented by a large online updating vocabulary with a target vocabulary sampling mechanism reduces the decoding time on CPUs up to 50% on P2C tasks with negligible change in conversion accuracy.
Sub-character tokenization improves computational efficiency and robustness to homophone typos in Chinese pretrained language models.
Character-based bidirectional LSTM-CRF with words and characters will outperform the state-of-the-art neural English named entity recognition model in Japanese.
Words, and not individual characters, are the unit of primary importance in Chinese reading.
The introduction of a Chinese corpus for fine-grained entity typing will enhance the performance of neural models on this task.
Creating embeddings for characters based on their visual characteristics will improve processing of instances with rare characters in languages such as Chinese, Japanese, and Korean.
The Named Entity task was carried out in Chinese and Japanese concurrently with English.
ChineseBERT incorporates both the glyph and pinyin information of Chinese characters into language model pretraining and yields significant performance boost over baseline models.

A convolutional neural network (CNN)-based method that incorporates lexicons using a rethinking mechanism will achieve better performance and be faster than both word-level and character-level baseline methods for Chinese named entity recognition.
The statistical approach to Pinyin-based Chinese input will improve typing accuracy and efficiency for Chinese PC users.
The character-based model for joint segmentation and POS tagging for Chinese will achieve state-of-the-art performance on CTB5, with an F1-score of 94.38.

The all-at-once, character-based approach is the best for Chinese part-of-speech tagging, while the one-at-a-time, character-based approach performs slightly worse in accuracy but takes shorter time to train and run.
Due to the limitation of current state-of-the-art Chinese word segmentation performance, a character-based name tagger can outperform its word-based counterparts for Chinese but not for Japanese.
The use of a 6-tag set, tone feature of Chinese character and assistant segmenters trained on other corpora further improve Chinese word segmentation performance.
Dynamic Connected Networks (DCN) will achieve a new state-of-the-art performance on Chinese spelling check tasks compared to existing BERT-based models.

Integrating subword embeddings trained using byte-pair encoding into a Lattice LSTM network significantly improves Chinese word segmentation performance compared to strong character-based segmentation models.
Training and evaluating named entity recognition models for Chinese biomedical patent data based on BERT will show the value and potential of this domain-specific NER task.
Confusionset-guided Pointer Networks for Chinese Spell Check will outperform all competitor models by a large margin of up to 20% F1 score.
The proposed approach to jointly embed Chinese words, characters, and fine-grained subcharacter components demonstrates superior performance on word similarity and word analogy tasks.

Charagram embeddings outperform more complex architectures based on character-level recurrent and convolutional neural networks, achieving new state-of-the-art performance on several similarity tasks.
cw2vec will outperform existing methods for learning Chinese word embeddings by exploiting stroke n-gram information.
The character-enhanced word embedding model (CWE) outperforms other baseline methods which ignore internal character information.
CHIME can handle typing errors in Chinese Pinyin input and achieves better performance than existing input methods.
Character-level Neural Machine Translation models will achieve promising results for Chinese-English translation by using the Wubi encoding scheme.
Incorporating the character-based subword module (char2subword) significantly improves the performance on the social media linguistic code-switching evaluation benchmark.

Glyph-aware embedding of Chinese characters will effectively incorporate the visual appearance of a character’s glyph in its representation, improving performance in language modeling and word segmentation tasks.
A character-level dependency scheme will effectively represent primary linguistic relationships within a Chinese sentence.

given_idea: Large language models can accurately assess online content without relying on human-annotated data, ensuring more efficient evaluation processes.
original: An unreferenced automated evaluation metric using large pre-trained language models will achieve higher correlation with human annotations in an online setting without requiring true responses for comparison during inference.

top_k.sentence: An unreferenced automated evaluation metric using large pre-trained language models will achieve higher correlation with human annotations in an online setting without requiring true responses for comparison during inference.
The automatic evaluation model based on conversational context will outperform existing evaluation metrics in terms of high correlation with human annotation scores.
Automatic content metrics for news summary evaluation can identify statistically significant differences present in manual evaluation of content.
Novel, system- and data-independent automatic evaluation methods for natural language generation (NLG) are needed due to the weak reflection of human judgments by existing metrics.
The proposed evaluation techniques will produce summary scores that replicate human assessments accurately without relying on gold-standard human summaries.
The evaluation protocol using a large set of automatic and human evaluation metrics will become the new evaluation standard when comparing neural generative models for text.
The Seq2SentiSeq model will outperform existing methods in fine-grained text sentiment transfer in both automatic evaluation and human evaluation.
This paper provides an overview of how human evaluation is currently conducted and presents a set of best practices, grounded in the literature.
For human evaluation of Natural Language Generation, correlation coefficients and agreement coefficients should be used together to obtain a better assessment of the evaluation data reliability.
Human evaluators do not correlate well with discriminative evaluators in ranking machine-generated text for online product reviews.
Automatic evaluation of single topics and whole topic models will improve the intrinsic quality assessment of topic models.
Automated summary evaluation based on the BLEU method will provide encouraging correlations with human judgments despite the instability of manual evaluation.
Item Response Theory will provide a more insightful evaluation of NLP systems compared to standard evaluation metrics.
Collecting human judgments for high-scoring summaries will resolve the debate over which evaluation metrics to trust.
Combining automatic metrics with human evaluation using control variates will yield an unbiased estimator with lower cost than human evaluation alone.
Reference-less grammaticality metrics correlate very strongly with human judgments and are competitive with the leading reference-based evaluation metrics.
The static attention based approach outperforms all the baselines on automatic and human evaluation.
A reader-focused evaluation should not be substituted for an expert-focused evaluation.
A novel statistical topic model based on an automated evaluation metric will significantly improve topic quality in a large-scale document collection from the National Institutes of Health.
Automatic evaluation of local explanations for text classification correlates moderately with human evaluation.
Information evaluation requires readers to set and monitor their goals of accuracy, coherence, and completeness, employ strategies to achieve these goals, and value systematic evaluation.
Best–worst scaling produces significantly more reliable results than the rating scale with the same total number of annotations.
Researchers tend to favour specific evaluation methods for end-to-end Natural Language Generation systems, and these methods are correlated with the publication venue.
Automatic evaluation using unigram co-occurrences between summary pairs correlates surprisingly well with human evaluations.
The two-level annotation scheme will effectively analyze consumer reviews for relevancy and evaluations.
Evaluating style transfer for text will benefit from standardized evaluation practices that correlate more strongly with human judgment.
The Automated Readability Index provides an easy, automated method for evaluating textual material in terms of readability.
Annotation schemes for natural language generation tasks aimed at evaluating language quality need to be treated with great care.

Evaluating response generation systems via response selection with the test set developed by our method correlates more strongly with human evaluation compared with widely used automatic evaluation metrics.
Learning an automatic scoring metric based on human judgments will improve the evaluation of summaries in the summarization field.
RankME significantly improves the reliability and consistency of human ratings compared to traditional evaluation methods.
Evaluation should focus on data efficiency and simple supervised tasks, where the amount of available data is varied and scores of a supervised model are reported for each subset.
Variation in human judgments affects the results and interpretation of text summarization evaluations.

The proposed evaluation measures, Reliability and Sensitivity, will provide a comprehensive assessment of document organization tasks, outperforming existing metrics in terms of formal constraints and empirical strictness.
The website and suite of offline tools facilitate evaluation of word vectors on standard lexical semantics benchmarks and permit exchange and archival by users.
CredEye system for automatic credibility assessment will analyze the credibility of natural language claims by considering relevant articles from the Web.
Pseudo-word evaluations should be conducted on the data in its entirety to improve selectional preference assessments.
The evaluation set was quite difficult to parse, particularly due to creative approaches to word representation in the web forum portion.
The proposed framework significantly outperforms other sequence-to-sequence based baseline models on both automatic and human evaluation.
Using only one rater can skew system evaluation and a sampling approach can evaluate a system more efficiently.

The framework for evaluating inference-rule resources simplifies the instance-based evaluation method, producing a large amount of annotations with high inter-annotator agreement for a low cost and short period of time.
The skeleton-based model can generate significantly more coherent text compared to state-of-the-art models according to human evaluation and automatic evaluation.
The dataset significantly highlights the gap between the best model and human performance on various evaluation metrics.
Different evaluators focus on different aspects of word models, and some are more correlated with natural language processing tasks.
Continuous rating scales for human evaluation in machine translation will improve intra-annotator and inter-annotator agreement compared to traditional methods.
The review of rating and Likert scales in Natural Language Generation evaluation tasks reveals deviations from good practice and provides recommendations for their appropriate use.
Current evaluation protocol is weakly correlated with human judgment and does not account for important characteristics such as factual correctness.

A machine learning algorithm that is not explicitly modeled to characterize evaluators' subjectivity is as reliable as the human evaluation in terms of assessing the most dominant experienced emotions.
Click-through rate may not be the optimal metric for online evaluation of recommender systems if producing relevant recommendations is of importance.
Word Embedding-Based automatic MT evaluation using Word Position Information (WE_WPI) achieves the highest correlation with human judgment among several representative metrics.

given_idea: The proposed approach will enable neural models to learn more accurate attention patterns, leading to substantial improvements in sentiment classification accuracy.
original: Progressive self-supervised attention learning approach for neural aspect-level sentiment classification models will yield better attention mechanisms, leading to substantial improvements over state-of-the-art models.

top_k.sentence: Progressive self-supervised attention learning approach for neural aspect-level sentiment classification models will yield better attention mechanisms, leading to substantial improvements over state-of-the-art models.
Attention-based bidirectional LSTM approach will improve target-dependent sentiment classification.

The iterative recursive attention model will identify and combine different aspects of input data in an interpretable manner while achieving performance close to the state of the art in sentiment classification.
An attention model will significantly improve the accuracy of target-dependent sentiment analysis by modeling the contribution of each word with respect to the target.
The lexicon-based supervised attention model (LBSA) allows a recurrent neural network to focus on the sentiment content, thus generating sentiment-informative representations.
