HunFlair outperforms other off-the-shelf biomedical named entity recognition tools with an average gain of 7.26 pp over the next best tool in a cross-corpus setting and achieves on-par results with state-of-the-art research prototypes in in-corpus experiments.
The newly created named entity recognisers are evaluated, with F-scores of between 0.64 and 0.77.
The neural Named Entity Recognition tool achieves around 77% micro-F1 performance on the SC-CoMIcs corpus, comparable to human annotator agreement rates.
Maximum entropy models will improve the accuracy of named entity recognition compared to baseline methods.

given_idea: Exclusive Supermask Subnetwork Training (ExSSNeT) will improve performance while preventing forgetting in Continual Learning compared to existing methods.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: The proposed method will alleviate catastrophic forgetting in continual learning with deep neural networks.
Sparse neural Network for Continual Learning (SNCL) achieves state-of-the-art result for mitigating forgetting.
Task-conditioned hypernetworks will significantly reduce catastrophic forgetting in continual learning by maintaining task-specific weight realizations.
Continual learning will enable artificial neural networks to accumulate knowledge over different tasks without catastrophic forgetting.
Experience Replay based framework ER-GNN will alleviate the catastrophic forgetting problem in existing Graph Neural Networks.
The less-forgetting learning method is effective in alleviating the catastrophic forgetting problem in deep neural networks.
Supervised Contrastive Replay (SCR) substantially reduces catastrophic forgetting and outperforms state-of-the-art continual learning methods by a significant margin on a variety of datasets.
The mechanisms that mitigate catastrophic forgetting in neural networks vary based on the incremental training paradigm and type of data being used.
Forgetting can in fact be favorable to learning in artificial neural networks.
Conditional channel gated networks will improve performance in continual learning tasks by preventing catastrophic forgetting.
A very small episodic memory in continual learning setup significantly outperforms specifically designed continual learning approaches.
Variational continual learning (VCL) outperforms state-of-the-art continual learning methods on a variety of tasks, avoiding catastrophic forgetting in a fully automatic way.
A strategy built on pseudo-labeling, consistency regularization, Out-of-Distribution detection, and knowledge distillation reduces forgetting in the semi-supervised continual learning setting.
Embedding networks suffer significantly less from catastrophic forgetting compared to classification networks.
The Supermasks in Superposition model can sequentially learn thousands of tasks without catastrophic forgetting.
Using experience replay buffers for all past events can substantially reduce catastrophic forgetting in continual learning tasks.
An expansion-based approach for task-free continual learning will effectively eliminate catastrophic forgetting by allocating new resources to learn new data.

A comprehensive experimental comparison of 10 state-of-the-art continual learning methods will determine which method performs best on balanced Tiny Imagenet and a large-scale unbalanced iNaturalist datasets.
Catastrophic forgetting occurs when connectionist networks learn new information, and by so doing, forget all previously learned information.
Intelligent synapses will dramatically reduce forgetting while maintaining computational efficiency in continual learning of classification tasks.
Simple baselines can achieve similar performance to current mainstream methods in continual learning scenarios.
Orthogonal Gradient Descent method will effectively restrict the direction of gradient updates to avoid catastrophic forgetting in neural networks during continual learning.
The proposed continual learning framework for neural machine translation models will alleviate the problem of catastrophic forgetting and improve performance compared to baseline models.
Adding multiple tasks to a single deep neural network while avoiding catastrophic forgetting will result in better robustness against catastrophic forgetting compared to prior work.

ARPER (Adaptively Regularized Prioritized Exemplar Replay) significantly outperforms other methods by effectively mitigating the detrimental catastrophic forgetting issue in continual learning for natural language generation.

The transformer architecture based on a dedicated encoder/decoder framework will efficiently reduce catastrophic forgetting in continual learning while scaling to a large number of tasks with negligible memory and time overheads.

The incremental learning algorithm for the Multilayer Perceptron will incorporate new knowledge without exhaustive re-training, achieving efficiency comparable to a network that is exhaustively trained.

The modular architecture and learning algorithm will perform competitively on widely used Continual Learning benchmarks while yielding superior performance on the more challenging benchmarks introduced in this work.

The proposed schemes MEGA-I and MEGA-II significantly advance the state-of-the-art on lifelong learning benchmarks, reducing the error by up to 18% compared to existing episodic memory based approaches.

Firefly neural architecture descent can flexibly grow networks both wider and deeper, and can be applied to learn accurate but resource-efficient neural architectures that avoid catastrophic forgetting in continual learning.
The task-based hard attention mechanism will effectively reduce catastrophic forgetting in neural networks, cutting current rates by 45 to 80%.
OML minimizes catastrophic interference by learning representations that accelerate future learning and are robust to forgetting under online updates in continual learning.
Continuing pre-training could lead to the pre-trained model’s catastrophic forgetting, and a learning method with less forgetting can alleviate this issue.
Controlled sampling of memories for replay will produce consistent gains in performance and greatly reduced forgetting in online continual learning.
Deep Generative Replay will alleviate catastrophic forgetting in continual learning by enabling the sampling of training data for previous tasks using a cooperative dual model architecture.
A dual-memory self-organizing architecture will significantly outperform current methods of lifelong learning in incremental learning scenarios.

The continual learning method for task-oriented dialogue systems with iterative network pruning, expanding, and masking will preserve performance on previously encountered tasks while accelerating learning progress on subsequent tasks.

Meta-Experience Replay (MER) will consistently outperform recently proposed baselines for continual learning across continual lifelong supervised learning benchmarks and non-stationary reinforcement learning environments.
Meta-learning with sparse experience replay will prevent catastrophic forgetting in lifelong language learning tasks.
The memory module can be easily added to any part of a supervised neural network, enhancing its ability to remember and perform life-long one-shot learning.
Dark Experience Replay will outperform consolidated approaches in General Continual Learning settings.
A novel continual learning approach can handle both learning and storing new information, as well as revisiting and adapting to changes in previously seen concepts.
Super-convergence allows neural networks to be trained an order of magnitude faster than with standard training methods.
Gradient Projection Memory (GPM) mitigates forgetting in neural networks by inducing minimum to no interference with past tasks through orthogonal gradient descent.

Augmenting an array of independent memory slots with a learnable random graph that captures pairwise similarities between samples will guard against catastrophic forgetting in continual learning models.
The CTR model will effectively address catastrophic forgetting and encourage knowledge transfer in continual learning.
An efficient meta-lifelong framework will achieve state-of-the-art performance using merely 1% memory size while alleviating both catastrophic forgetting and negative transfer.
Episodic memory activation and reconsolidation (EMAR) will prevent catastrophic forgetting of old relations and outperform state-of-the-art continual learning models.
Teaching strategies that leverage the underlying forgetting mechanisms of the learners will optimize the expected performance of forgetful learners.
Learning binary masks allows for adding multiple tasks to a single, fixed deep neural network without affecting performance on already learned tasks.

given_idea: Q-Diversity can consistently improve worst-case accuracy under different distributional shifts, outperforming state-of-the-art alternatives.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: The proposed distribution matching approach consistently outperforms previous methods by a large margin.
Standard training yields substantially lower out-of-distribution than in-distribution performance on datasets reflecting distribution shifts.

Jackknifing method will yield approximately normally distributed estimates for an index of diversity and provide estimated standard deviations for hypothesis testing and confidence interval estimates.
The discrete key-value bottleneck minimizes the effect of learning under distribution shifts and reduces the complexity of the hypothesis class.
For any fixed target function, there exists a distribution weighted combining rule that has a loss of at most ∊ with respect to any target mixture of the source distributions.
f-DPG allows the use of any f-divergence to approximate any target distribution that can be evaluated.
Welch’s t-test provides a better control of Type 1 error rates when the assumption of homogeneity of variance is not met.
Kernel herding decreases the error of expectations of functions in the Hilbert space at a rate O(1/T), which is much faster than the usual O(1/pT) for iid random samples.
Deterministic uncertainty quantification (DUQ) can reliably detect out of distribution data points at test time with a single forward pass.
Skew-Fit converges to a uniform distribution over the set of valid states, even when we do not know this set beforehand.
Diversity Training significantly improves the adversarial robustness of ensembles and can be combined with existing methods to create a stronger defense.

Q-AMSGradR algorithm will outperform the vanilla Q-learning with stochastic gradient descent updates and demonstrate significantly better performance than the DQN learning method over a batch of Atari 2600 games.
ID-calibrated ensembles outperform prior state-of-the-art on both in-distribution and out-of-distribution accuracy.
Out-of-distribution performance is strongly correlated with in-distribution performance for a wide range of models and distribution shifts.
Counterfactually augmented data may limit the model's effectiveness on out-of-distribution generalization due to lack of perturbation diversity.
A maximum-selection algorithm using (ε, δ)-PAC will perform optimally in terms of the number of comparisons required for general probabilistic models.
Quadapter effectively prevents overfitting and improves quantization performance for GPT-2.
The CorrRNN model will significantly outperform the state-of-the-art method on benchmark datasets in terms of both accuracy and diversity.
Promoting ensemble diversity via an adaptive diversity promoting regularizer will improve adversarial robustness for ensemble models while maintaining state-of-the-art accuracy on normal examples.
The choice of function Q associated with the simulated annealing algorithm must be as large as possible to provide a fast rate of convergence.
Model variations do not affect robustness and in-distribution performance alone determines out-of-distribution performance.
An estimator based on stronger structural assumptions will provide faster rates of convergence for estimating f-divergences between probability distributions.

Algorithms for distributionally robust optimization of convex losses with conditional value at risk and chi-squared divergence uncertainty sets require a number of gradient evaluations independent of training set size and number of parameters.
Under total variation distance corruptions, the breakdown point of the Tukey median estimator reduces to 1/4 for high-dimensional halfspace-symmetric distributions.
Energy scores better distinguish in- and out-of-distribution samples than the traditional approach using the softmax scores.

The probabilistic approach based on the principle of maximum entropy provides a well-defined, globally normalized distribution over decision sequences while offering the same performance guarantees as existing methods.

The Kronecker factored Laplace approximation leads to better uncertainty estimates on out-of-distribution data and is more robust to simple adversarial attacks compared to Dropout and a diagonal Laplace approximation.
The inclusion of both training and inference diversity terms improves performance, out-performing current state-of-the-art systems in terms of ROUGE scores.
Distributionally robust optimization (DRO) controls the risk of the minority group at each time step while remaining oblivious to the identity of the groups.
No out-of-distribution detection method is inherently better than others on a standardized set of in-distribution and out-of-distribution pairs.
Increasing both the training set and model sizes significantly improve the distributional shift robustness.
Models trained with the resulting parametric adversaries are consistently more robust to subpopulation shifts when compared to other Distributionally Robust Optimization approaches.
Composable core-sets will enable efficient solutions to diversity and coverage maximization problems in massive data processing applications.
The validity of an algorithm whose running time goes up slightly faster than the square of d is proven.
The proposed algorithms guarantee query times that are sub-linear in n and only polynomial in the diversity parameter k, as well as the dimension d.

This paper develops desiderata for probabilistic optimization algorithms and presents a concrete algorithm which addresses each of the computational intractabilities with a sequence of approximations.
A probability distribution is defined over the r! permutations of r objects in such a way as to incorporate up to r! -1 parameters.
The shortcut mitigation framework LGTR can improve the generalization accuracy on out-of-distribution data while preserving the accuracy on in-distribution data.
The (k,k?) nearest neighbor rule with a reject option has an error rate bounded in terms of the Bayes' error rate.
A combination of local and global models reduces both variance in the data as well as variance across device distributions.

KL regularization within an approximate value iteration scheme implicitly averages q-values and provides a strong performance bound with linear dependency to the horizon and an error propagation term involving an averaging effect.

An Occam-style phase space argument expands the priors into their infinite mixture and resolves most of the observed problems, leading to a surprisingly good estimator of entropies of discrete distributions.
The novel method of over-sampling candidates and using clustering to remove similar sequences will achieve high diversity without sacrificing quality in outputs from conditional language models.
GEORGE will boost worst-case subclass accuracy by up to 22 percentage points compared to standard training techniques without requiring any information about the subclasses.
The proposed model leads to significant improvement on both relevance and diversity over state-of-the-art baselines.
Inverse correlations between in-distribution and out-of-distribution performance do happen in real-world data.
Non-parametric nearest-neighbor distance for out-of-distribution detection will provide stronger flexibility and generality compared to prior methods that impose distributional assumptions.
Reinforcing Determinantal Point Process (R-DPP) performs better on accuracy and diversity than using noise as a control signal.
Using optimized distributions for backing-off in stochastic language modeling will improve performance, resulting in a 10% reduction in perplexity and a 5% reduction in word error rate.
The differentiable successive halving method of relaxing the top-k operator achieves a better approximation of top-k and lower computational cost compared to the previous approach.

given_idea: Open-Modality Speech Recognition (OpenSR) enables modality transfer from one to any in three different settings (zero-, few- and full-shot) and achieves highly competitive zero-shot performance compared to the existing few-shot and full-shot lip-reading methods.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: The new approach for zero-shot cross-modal transfer between speech and text significantly improves the state-of-the-art for zero-shot speech translation.
Cross-modal distillation from an Automatic Speech Recognition model enables training of lip reading systems without requiring human annotated ground truth data.
Discrete Cross-Modal Alignment method will significantly improve zero-shot speech translation performance compared to existing methods.
Leveraging visual modality-specific representations will enhance the performance of audio-visual speech recognition in noisy conditions compared to traditional modality-invariant approaches.
Multimodal one-shot learning will enable a robot to match new continuous speech input to the correct visual instance after seeing one paired audiovisual example per class.

Mapping the sequence of mouth movement images directly to mel-spectrogram will reconstruct speech relevant information and outperform state-of-the-art models on the GRID benchmark lip reading dataset.
WavPrompt is a few-shot learner that can perform speech understanding tasks better than a naive text baseline.
An attention-based pooling mechanism and sub-word units for lip reading will significantly improve the performance of lip reading models in recognizing speech from silent videos.

Learning visual speech Representations from Audio via self-supervision (LiRA) will significantly outperform other self-supervised methods on the Lip Reading in the Wild (LRW) dataset and achieve state-of-the-art performance on Lip Reading Sentences 2 (LRS2) using only a fraction of the total labelled data.
The zero-shot method for audio-visual keyword spotting improves performance in identifying spoken words in videos, with or without audio.
GenerSpeech will surpass the state-of-the-art models in terms of audio quality and style similarity for zero-shot style transfer of out-of-domain custom voice.
Audio-Visual Hidden Unit BERT (AV-HuBERT) learns powerful audio-visual speech representation benefiting both lip-reading and automatic speech recognition.
The audio-visual speech recognition system will significantly reduce error rates in noisy environments compared to traditional acoustic-only methods.
SpeechGPT has an impressive capacity to follow multi-modal human instructions and highlights the potential of handling multiple modalities with one model.
Leveraging unimodal self-supervised learning will promote the multimodal audio-visual speech recognition.
The proposed end-to-end, multitask learning audiovisual automatic speech recognition system will achieve the best performance across all conditions for both speech tasks.
openSMILE 2.0 unites feature extraction paradigms from speech, music, and general sound events with basic video features for multi-modal processing.
Jointly modeling automatic speech recognition tokens and visual features results in higher performance compared to training individually on either modality.
A novel collaborative framework for lip reading will outperform state-of-the-art methods by effectively distilling knowledge from a master network that integrates audio and video modalities.
Lip by Speech (LIBS) will improve lip reading performance by distilling knowledge from speech recognizers.

The developed system of face- and neck-worn sensors and algorithms will achieve a recognition rate of 91.1% for subvocal speech, demonstrating its viability as an alternative modality of communication.

The two-stage model (audio enhanced multi-modality speech recognition, AE-MSR) consistently achieves the state-of-the-art performance by a significant margin, demonstrating the necessity and effectiveness of AE-MSR.

The self-supervised audio-visual speech recognition framework built upon Audio-Visual HuBERT outperforms prior state-of-the-art by ~50% using less than 10% of labeled data in the presence of babble noise.
Language models trained on the Interview dataset exhibit better zero-shot out-of-domain performance on existing spoken dialog datasets.

Bimodal deep fusion state machine network will improve the robustness of audio-visual speech recognition in noisy environments through optimal integration of acoustic and visual information, multi-condition training, and dropout regularization.
Novel end-to-end multimodal Automatic Speech Recognition systems will perform better than adaptive training approaches, although improvements are slightly less.

Custom sequence loss training is the state-of-the-art on open spoken language understanding datasets and leads to 6% relative improvement in both automatic speech recognition and natural language understanding performance metrics on large proprietary datasets.

The proposed zero-shot voice transfer method via disentangled representation learning outperforms other baselines and obtains state-of-the-art results in terms of transfer accuracy and voice naturalness for voice style transfer experiments.
Multi-head Visual-audio Memory (MVM) will effectively distinguish homophenes and improve lip reading performance by utilizing audio representations to complement visual information.
Optimal Completion Distillation (OCD) achieves state-of-the-art performance on end-to-end speech recognition.
Training on the Gutenberg dialogue dataset achieves better response quality in zero-shot and finetuning settings compared to the larger but noisier Opensubtitles dataset.
A cross-modal student-teacher learning framework will improve speech recognition performance under low signal-to-noise-ratio and acoustic mismatch conditions.
The multimodal sparse transformer network will improve recognition performance in audio-visual speech recognition compared to existing methods.
Large-scale weak supervision will enable speech processing systems to generalize well to standard benchmarks in a zero-shot transfer setting without the need for fine-tuning.
The visual contribution to oral speech intelligibility is independent of the speech-to-noise ratio under test.
Learning both an acoustic model and a word/image association from multi-modal co-occurrences between speech and pictures will be effective.
SpecAugment will achieve state-of-the-art performance on automatic speech recognition tasks compared to prior methods.
An audio-visual corpus will support speech perception and automatic speech recognition studies.
The adaptive combination of visual and semantic modalities will significantly improve performance in few-shot learning compared to current uni-modality and modality-alignment methods.
Multimodal deep learning will improve feature learning across modalities, leading to better performance in tasks such as audio-visual speech classification.

Pre-training an audio-text multimodal model with extremely low-resource parallel data and extra non-parallel unimodal data will achieve comparable performance on multiple downstream speech understanding tasks compared with the model pre-trained on fully parallel data.
End-to-end training, the use of a conformer, and the use of a transformer-based language model significantly improve the performance of the model in audio-visual speech recognition.

The Predict-and-Update Network (P&U net) outperforms the state-of-the-art Audio-Visual Speech Recognition (AVSR) methods on both LRS2-BBC and LRS3-BBC datasets, with the relative reduced Word Error Rates (WERs) exceeding 10% and 40% under clean and noisy conditions, respectively.
The audio-visual fusion strategy will enhance recognition accuracy in both clean and noisy conditions compared to the acoustic modality alone.
Wav-BERT significantly outperforms existing approaches and achieves state-of-the-art performance on low-resource speech recognition.
Monotonic Chunkwise Attention (MoChA) allows online and linear-time decoding at test time while achieving state-of-the-art results in online speech recognition.
Learning accurate lip sequences to speech mappings for individual speakers in unconstrained, large vocabulary settings will achieve accurate, natural lip to speech synthesis.

The proposed method can effectively fuse speech and text information into one model, achieving between 1.7 and 2.3 BLEU improvement above the state of the art on the MuST-C speech translation dataset and comparable WERs to wav2vec 2.0 on the Librispeech speech recognition task.
Learning modality-invariant representations for speech and images will effectively filter out modality information while preserving semantic information.
The unified speech-language pre-trained model will outperform conventional cascaded methods and state-of-the-art approaches to end-to-end spoken language understanding with much less paired data.

given_idea: Instruction tuning can achieve superior results in zero-shot and few-shot scenarios and obtain the best performance on the fully-supervised tasks in StoryWars.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Instruction tuning substantially improves zero-shot performance on unseen tasks for language models.
MultiInstruct will improve zero-shot performance on multimodal tasks through instruction tuning.
The proposed framework learns to reliably accomplish program instructions and achieves zero-shot generalization to more complex instructions.

Using GPT-4 to generate instruction-following data for large language model finetuning leads to superior zero-shot performance on new tasks compared to instruction-following data generated by previous state-of-the-art models.
Training instruction-following agents with deep reinforcement learning will achieve robust performance with natural human instructions through zero-shot transfer from synthetic template commands.
The proposed zero-shot learning model can achieve state-of-the-art results on both zero-shot learning and few-shot learning problems.
The unified approach for conventional zero-shot, generalized zero-shot, and few-shot learning problems will consistently achieve superior performance compared to prevalent techniques.
VIMA will outperform alternative designs in zero-shot generalization settings by achieving a task success rate up to 2.9 times higher given the same training data.
Zero-shot learners will achieve state-of-the-art performance on natural language understanding tasks with significantly fewer parameters compared to existing models.
Instruction tuning is the key to the large language model’s zero-shot summarization capability.
Meta-tuning directly optimizes the zero-shot learning objective by fine-tuning pre-trained language models on a collection of datasets.
The few-shot learning ability on unseen tasks can be improved via an upstream learning stage using a set of seen tasks.
Meta-learning applied to multi-task instructional learning will further improve generalization to unseen tasks in a zero-shot setting.
Tip-Adapter performs comparably to training-required approaches for few-shot classification while inheriting the training-free advantage of zero-shot CLIP.
Few-shot Learning can rapidly generalize to new tasks containing only a few samples with supervised information.
Prompt-based learners can successfully be applied in true few-shot settings.
A comprehensive survey is given on the methodology, implementation, and fair evaluations for practical and applied computing facets on the recent progress of zero-shot learning.
The proposed method outperforms previous methods by a large margin in interactive instruction-following tasks.
Dual context-guided continuous prompt tuning method will outperform existing prompt tuning methods in few-shot learning tasks.
Z-ICL significantly outperforms previous zero-shot methods and is on par with in-context learning with labeled training data in the few-shot setting.
Models fine-tuned on our augmented data mixed with few-shot ground truth data are able to approach or surpass existing state-of-the-art performance on both datasets.
A modified baseline method achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art few-shot learning algorithms on both the miniImageNet and the CUB datasets.
AdaPrompt can improve over standard pre-trained language models in few-shot settings and outperforms standard prompt-based methods by up to 26.35% relative error reduction in zero-shot settings.

Using both types of pretrained language models for fully zero-shot learning of natural language understanding tasks will significantly outperform zero-shot prompting methods and achieve comparable results to strong few-shot approaches.
Pre-trained prompt tuning (PPT) can reach or even outperform full-model fine-tuning under both full-data and few-shot settings.
InstructionNER will outperform other baselines on few-shot named entity recognition tasks.

The instruction-tuned model outperforms single and multi-task baselines on an average by ~23% and ~6% across various evaluation settings, and more importantly, instruction-tuned model outperforms baselines by ~5% on cross-tasks.
RetroPrompt can obtain better performance in both few-shot and zero-shot settings and can yield better generalization abilities with new datasets.
Zemi, a zero-shot semi-parametric language model, demonstrates strong zero-shot performance on a wide range of held-out unseen tasks.
Mapping raw visual observations and text input to actions for instruction execution will significantly improve performance over supervised learning and common reinforcement learning variants.
Combining the few-shot setting with the above improvements decreases the initial 30-point accuracy gap between the zero-shot and the full-data settings by two thirds.
A mining-based approach for zero-shot learning will outperform prompting language models on a wide range of tasks.
Scaling to 1,000 tasks can substantially improve training efficiency and zero-shot generalization performance.
PanGu-α will demonstrate superior capabilities in performing various tasks under few-shot or zero-shot settings compared to existing large-scale pretrained language models.
VideoCLIP will achieve state-of-the-art performance in zero-shot video and text understanding tasks compared to prior work and supervised approaches.
SOLOIST creates new state-of-the-art results on two well-known benchmarks, CamRest and MultiWOZ, and significantly outperforms existing methods in the few-shot learning setting.
HyperCLOVA will show state-of-the-art in-context zero-shot and few-shot learning performances on various downstream tasks in Korean.
Continual PostTraining will improve few-shot end-task learning in domain-specific applications.
Modeling zero-shot performance prediction as a multi-task learning problem will lead to more accurate predictors for tasks with limited test data.
Lite Prompted Self-Training improves model performance for few-shot learning while efficiently fine-tuning a small number of parameters.
Automated curriculum learning minimizes worst-case-aware losses across tasks, leading to better performance in zero-shot and few-shot transfer settings.
The zero-shot framework can be applied to new unseen event types without additional manual annotations and achieved performance comparable to a supervised model.
The zero-shot system achieves a new state-of-the-art on the English dataset used in the evaluation.
Unified Prompt Tuning (UPT) achieves a better trade-off than the unimodal counterparts on few-shot learning benchmarks and domain generalization benchmarks.
Continual training of an instruction-following agent from user feedback will improve instruction execution accuracy over time.
Instance-aware prompt learning method learns a different prompt for each instance and achieves state-of-the-art results on the SuperGLUE few-shot learning benchmark.
A learning approach focused on recovery from cascading errors between instructions will improve the mapping of user instructions to system actions in collaborative scenarios.
FlipDA achieves a good tradeoff between effectiveness and robustness in few-shot natural language understanding tasks.

Training on Unnatural Instructions rivals the effectiveness of training on open-source manually-curated datasets and surpasses the performance of models such as T0++ and Tk-Instruct across various benchmarks.
Unlabeled Data Augmented Instruction Tuning (UDIT) will improve instruction tuning performance by effectively utilizing unlabeled data.

given_idea: The classifier decomposition framework will consistently outperform state-of-the-art continual relation extraction models.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Employing relation prototypes to extract useful information of each relation enhances continual relation extraction performance.
The Frustratingly Easy but Effective Approach (FEA) method achieves comparable or superior performance compared with the state-of-the-art baselines for continual relation extraction.
The proposed approach based on lifelong learning and optimization-based meta-learning will markedly outperform current state-of-the-art approaches in lifelong relation extraction.
A new paradigm of Relation Extraction that considers the predictions of all relations in the same context will outperform state-of-the-art approaches.
The Continual Relation Extraction framework with Contrastive Learning (CRECL) will alleviate the catastrophic forgetting problem and improve performance in continual relation extraction tasks.
Curriculum-meta learning method will effectively tackle catastrophic forgetting and order-sensitivity challenges in continual relation extraction.
A globally optimized neural model for end-to-end relation extraction will achieve better performance compared to local classification methods.
A framework that unifies the learning of Relation Extraction and Knowledge Base Embeddings models leads to significant improvements over the state-of-the-art in Relation Extraction.
The discriminative reasoning framework will outperform the previous state-of-the-art performance on the large-scale document-level relation extraction dataset.
The proposed document-level relation extraction model with iterative inference will outperform existing methods in terms of accuracy and efficiency.
The generative model for relation extraction and classification (GREC) will achieve state-of-the-art performance on benchmark relation extraction datasets.
Using only basic unit features is generally sufficient to achieve state-of-the-art performance in relation extraction.

Leveraging full dependency forests for relation extraction will provide significant improvements over existing methods, demonstrating state-of-the-art or competitive performances on biomedical and newswire benchmarks.
The Richer-but-Smarter shortest dependency path (RbSP) model will improve performance in relation extraction compared to existing methods.
The proposed encoder-classifier-reconstructor model for document-level relation extraction will significantly improve the accuracy of relation extraction compared to existing graph-based models.
The combination of better supervised data and a more appropriate high-capacity model enables much better relation extraction performance.
The proposed methods achieve better performance than the baseline systems in both automatic seed selection and noise reduction for relation extraction.
The new approach to relation extraction can reliably extract relations from web documents using minimal supervision.
OneRel will perform better than state-of-the-art baselines in joint entity and relation extraction tasks.
Incorporating relation paths in neural relation extraction will achieve significant and consistent improvements on relation extraction compared to existing methods.
The joint embedding model will achieve significant improvement on relation extraction compared to cutting-edge methods.
The span-level model will achieve state-of-the-art performance in relation extraction tasks compared to token-level models.
The simple baseline approach (PARE) outperforms existing state-of-the-art distantly supervised relation extraction models in both monolingual and multilingual datasets.
The walk-based model on entity graphs for relation extraction achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art systems on the ACE 2005 dataset without using any external tools.
The proposed framework will achieve a new state-of-the-art performance in terms of both AUC and top-n precision metrics for distantly supervised relation extraction.
Combining distant and partial supervision for relation extraction will yield a substantial increase in end-to-end F1 score on the 2013 KBP Slot Filling evaluation.
The proposed approach is scalable and can perform state-of-the-art on the standard benchmark ACE 2005 for multiple relation extractions.
The multi-lingual neural relation extraction framework will achieve significant improvements on relation extraction compared to existing methods.
The proposed adversarial training mechanism for relation extraction can effectively filter out noisy instances and achieve significant improvements compared with the state-of-the-art models.
Exploiting syntactico-semantic structures for relation extraction will provide significant improvement in relation extraction performance.
A joint approach for entity-level relation extraction using multi-instance learning will achieve state-of-the-art results and be more efficient compared to task-specific learning.
The proposed SSAN model significantly outperforms competitive baselines in document-level relation extraction tasks.
The dependency tree kernel achieves a 20% F1 improvement over a 'bag-of-words' kernel for relation extraction.
REBEL, a seq2seq model based on BART, performs end-to-end relation extraction for more than 200 different relation types and attains state-of-the-art performance in most benchmarks.
A sequence-to-sequence approach for document-level relation extraction will outperform pipeline-based methods in terms of performance and efficiency.
The contrastive instance learning framework will significantly improve the performance of distantly supervised relation extraction models compared to the multi-instance learning framework.
The shortest path dependency kernel outperforms a recent approach based on dependency tree kernels for relation extraction.
Generating knowledge-infused relation prototypes will leverage the relational knowledge from pre-trained language models to improve continual relation extraction.

A simple pipelined approach for entity and relation extraction will establish the new state-of-the-art on standard benchmarks, obtaining a 1.7%-2.8% absolute improvement in relation F1 over previous joint models.
Relation Extraction with Distant Supervision and Transformers (REDSandT) captures a broader set of relations with higher confidence compared to existing approaches.
The semi-supervised relation extraction system with large-scale word clustering outperformed a state-of-the-art supervised baseline system.
The dependency-driven approach for relation extraction with attentive graph convolutional networks (A-GCN) outperforms previous studies and achieves state-of-the-art performance on both datasets.
WebKE will outperform state-of-the-art methods in relation extraction from semi-structured web content.
Task agnostic relation representations significantly outperform previous work on exemplar based relation extraction.
The path-based representation learning model will achieve significant and consistent improvements on knowledge base completion and relation extraction from text compared to existing methods.
A convolutional neural network for relation extraction will significantly outperform traditional relation extraction systems that rely on linguistic analysis features.
Element Intervention will outperform previous state-of-the-art methods in unsupervised relation extraction and is robust across different datasets.
The proposed model achieves superior performance on two public datasets for document-level relation extraction.
Distant supervision allows relation extraction without labeled data, achieving a precision of 67.6% for 10,000 instances of 102 relations.
Adaptive thresholding and localized context pooling will improve document-level relation extraction performance compared to existing models.

given_idea: Drift in the classifier leads to forgetting in class-incremental learning.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: The system should incrementally learn new classes round by round without re-training on the examples of preceding classes and should perform well on new classes without much loss on preceding classes.
The mechanisms that mitigate catastrophic forgetting in neural networks vary based on the incremental training paradigm and type of data being used.

A causal framework can explain the catastrophic forgetting in Class-Incremental Learning and the proposed causal effect distillation can improve various state-of-the-art Class-Incremental Learning methods by a large margin (0.72%–9.06%).
