The Multimodal Transformer model will outperform state-of-the-art methods in modeling unaligned multimodal language sequences.
Hindi Visual Genome is a multimodal dataset suitable for English-Hindi multimodal machine translation task and multimodal research.
A multimodal approach to explanation provides complementary explanatory strengths and yields better justification models and evidence localization compared to unimodal approaches.
The novel modality-merging method will mitigate the modality collapse issue in the multimodal architecture for attribute extraction.
UNIMO can effectively adapt to both single-modal and multi-modal understanding and generation tasks.
M6: Multi-Modality-to-Multi-Modality Multitask Mega-transformer will outperform the baseline in a number of downstream tasks concerning both single modality and multiple modalities.
The performance of task-specific fusion-based multimodal classifiers drops by 23.3% and 22.5% in the presence of dilutions generated by our model.

Multimodal machine translation will benefit from incorporating additional modalities such as audio and visual inputs, leading to improved performance in tasks like spoken language translation and image-guided translation.
This position paper maps out a conceptual framework to address the gap regarding implicitness, ambiguity, and underspecification in multimodal image+text systems.

The model optimizes for a joint generative-discriminative objective across multimodal data and labels and is able to learn meaningful multimodal representations that achieve state-of-the-art or competitive performance on six multimodal datasets.

Multimodal word distributions formed from Gaussian mixtures will capture uniquely expressive semantic information and outperform alternatives, such as word2vec skip-grams and Gaussian embeddings, on benchmark datasets.
Learning robust joint representations by translating between modalities will improve performance in multimodal sentiment analysis while maintaining robustness to missing or perturbed modalities.
The supervised multimodal bitransformer model fuses information from text and image encoders and obtains state-of-the-art performance on various multimodal classification benchmark tasks.
Text-Image Modality Adversarial Matching approach learns modality-invariant feature representations using adversarial and cross-modal matching objectives.

The proposed method can successfully measure visual knowledge transfer capabilities in models and the novel model architecture shows promising results for leveraging multimodal knowledge in a unimodal setting.
Regularization by maximizing functional entropies will balance the contribution of each modality to the classification result in multi-modal classifiers.
Multimodal information bottleneck (MIB) aims to learn a powerful and sufficient multimodal representation that is free of redundancy and to filter out noisy information in unimodal representations.
Multimodal deep learning will improve feature learning across modalities, leading to better performance in tasks such as audio-visual speech classification.
Multimodal models better reconstruct attribute distributions, but are still subject to reporting bias.
The Multimodal Transformer model will significantly outperform previous state-of-the-art methods in image captioning by capturing both intra- and inter-modal interactions.
Multimodal Compact Bilinear pooling method will show improvements in multimodal image caption translation compared to basic combination methods.

given_idea: Tree structure Reasoning schEmA (TREA) will clarify the causal relationships between mentioned entities and generate more reasonable and suitable responses for recommended results in conversational recommender systems.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Incorporating both word-oriented and entity-oriented knowledge graphs will enhance the data representations in conversational recommender systems, leading to improved performance on recommendation and conversation tasks.
The topic-guided conversational recommender system will improve the effectiveness of item recommendations through natural semantic transitions in conversations.
Tree-enhanced Embedding Method will combine the strengths of embedding-based and tree-based models to provide superior performance and explainability in personalized recommendation.

Conversational Path Reasoning (CPR) will significantly outperform existing conversational recommendation system methods in terms of effectiveness by utilizing user preferred attributes in an explicit way.
Neural logic reasoning for explainable recommendation (LOGER) will deliver high-quality recommendations while ensuring the faithfulness of the derived explanations.
Modeling latent tree structures results in superior performance in natural entailment detection and answer sentence selection.
An end-to-end graph-based reasoning model will effectively update user memory and predict optimal dialog policies for conversational recommendation.
Conversational recommender systems (CRS) support a richer set of interactions that can improve the preference elicitation process.

Dual Imitation for Conversational Recommendation (DICR) achieves bidirectional promotion between recommendation and conversation modules, generating high-quality responses with accurate recommendations and coherent explanations.
The multi-turn question answering paradigm for entity-relation extraction significantly outperforms previous best models on the ACE and CoNLL04 corpora.
Topic-guided Conversational Recommender (TCR) will achieve superior performance in multi-domain settings compared to existing conversational recommendation methods.
RLET will improve the generation of entailment trees in explainable question answering by utilizing reinforcement learning to accumulate training signals across the whole tree.
Explainable conversational recommendation enables incremental improvement of both recommendation accuracy and explanation quality through multi-turn user-model conversation.
Generating explanations in the form of entailment trees will enhance understanding and debugging of the reasoning process in open-domain textual question-answering.
Complex Question Answering over Knowledge Base aims to automatically answer natural language questions via well-structured relation information between entities stored in knowledge bases.

The hierarchical reinforcement learning framework enhances the interaction between entity mentions and relation types, leading to better performance in extracting overlapping relations compared to existing methods.

Modeling document-level causal structures for achieving comprehensive causal relation identification will significantly improve the performance of causal relation identification, especially in identifying cross-sentence causal relations.
Hierarchical Information-aware Conversational Recommender (HICR) will boost the performance of conversational recommendation systems by modeling hierarchical information.
The method for the automatic detection and extraction of causal relations will improve the performance of question answering systems.
GlobalGraph will effectively model long-distance node relations in Knowledge Base Question Answering by utilizing node type similarity and correlation between nodes and questions.
Leveraging historical interaction data will improve the performance of conversational recommender systems.
Review-augmented Conversational Recommender (RevCore) will yield better performance on both recommendation and conversation responding compared to existing conversational recommendation systems.
Policy-Guided Path Reasoning (PGPR) couples recommendation and interpretability by providing actual paths in a knowledge graph.
QUD trees can replace discourse relations in the representation of naturalistic data.
Structural relationships between questions and their candidate answer passages can significantly improve the accuracy of answer re-ranking models in question answering systems.
The Estimation-Action-Reflection framework will significantly improve the interaction between conversational and recommender systems compared to existing methods.

Dynamic Explainable Recommender (DER) will improve recommendation performance and provide explanations tailored for users’ current preferences compared to existing static explainable recommender models.
The Stepwise Reasoning Network will outperform state-of-the-art approaches in multi-relation question answering over knowledge graphs.
Tree edit models will effectively model pairs of semantically related sentences for recognizing textual entailment, paraphrase identification, and answer selection.

Integrating research in dialog systems and recommender systems into a unified deep reinforcement learning framework will build a personalized conversational recommendation agent that optimizes a per session based utility function.
Conversational recommendation can effectively transition from non-recommendation dialogs to recommendation dialogs by considering user interests and feedback.
The proposed method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods and enhances the scalability and stability of conversational recommender systems.
CoArse-to-FinE neural symbolic reasoning approach (CAFE) will achieve better recommendation performance and generate explanations for recommendations compared to existing methods.
SciDTB uses dependency trees to represent discourse structure, which is flexible and simplified but does not sacrifice structural integrity.
An item recommendation framework which jointly models the full spectrum of interactions will effectively exploit monotonic dependency structures in feedback signals.
Modeling latent relations between mentions in entity linking will improve the performance of the entity-linking system.
In a large knowledge base, reasoning patterns required to answer a query type reoccur for various entities in their respective subgraph neighborhoods.

The proposed GraphRec framework will effectively address the challenges in building social recommender systems by jointly capturing interactions and opinions in the user-item graph and coherently modeling user-user and user-item graphs.

The Interpretable Reasoning Network yields state-of-the-art results on multi-relation question answering tasks and allows for traceable and observable intermediate predictions for reasoning analysis and failure diagnosis.
Modeling transitions of focal entities will improve the effectiveness of conversational knowledge base question answering.
Explainable recommendation improves the transparency, persuasiveness, effectiveness, trustworthiness, and satisfaction of recommendation systems.
Empowering a relation extraction method with additional evidence from Wikipedia will improve the performance of knowledge-based question answering systems.
The integration of a predicate-oriented latent graph will improve conversational semantic role labeling performance compared to existing methods.
The proposed framework will exploit more valid facts while obtaining explainability for multi-hop question answering by dynamically constructing a semantic graph and reasoning over it.
The framework compares two representative conversational models, ChatGPT and Galactica, against Knowledge Graph Question Answering Network, the current state-of-the-art Question Answering system.
The dialogue-level mixed dependency graph (D2G) and argument reasoning graph (ARG) will improve relation extraction performance by effectively utilizing syntactic and discourse structures.
The QUD-tree framework allows for reasonable agreement regarding discourse structure and good agreement regarding the annotation of information structure.
A collective inference method that simultaneously resolves a set of mentions will effectively address the challenges of entity linking for tweets.

Two-phase hypergraph based reasoning with dynamic relations will effectively improve multi-hop knowledge base question answering by dynamically updating relation information and predicting relations hop-by-hop.
The graph reasoning network (GRN) can dramatically outperform strong baseline methods and achieve performance close to human-level in multi-turn response selection for retrieval-based chatbots.

given_idea: Cycle training achieves nearly the same performance as fully supervised approaches for the data-to-text generation task on the WebNLG, E2E, WTQ, and WSQL datasets.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: This survey offers a consolidated view into the neural data-to-text generation paradigm with a structured examination of the approaches, benchmark datasets, and evaluation protocols.
The proposed few-shot approach for neural data-to-text generation will outperform fully supervised sequence-to-sequence models with less than 10% of the training set.
A two-stage approach with a delayed copy mechanism will improve the precision of data records in generated texts compared to existing data-to-text generation methods.
The novel approach to data-to-text generation based on iterative text editing maximizes the completeness and semantic accuracy of the output text.
The data-to-text generation model with tracking and generation modules outperforms existing models in all evaluation metrics, and incorporating writer information further improves performance.
The trainable end-to-end generation system that includes both content selection and ordering will yield considerable improvements over the state of the art for generating text from a database.
Entity-centric neural architecture for data-to-text generation will outperform competitive baselines in automatic and human evaluation.
The proposed neural model enhanced with a planning component will outperform strong baselines in data-to-text generation tasks.
The proposed objective can approximate or even surpass popular data augmentation methods in text generation tasks.
Graph-to-sequence model will outperform Seq2Seq and Tree2Seq baselines in SQL-to-text generation tasks.
TableGPT will outperform existing systems in few-shot table-to-text generation across various datasets.

Generating text by transforming single-item descriptions with a sequence of modules trained on general-domain text-based operations enables data-to-text generation from RDF triples in zero-shot settings.
Changing the order of training samples in curriculum learning improves performance and convergence speed in neural data-to-text generation.
Any-Shot Data-to-Text (ASDOT) will achieve significant improvement over baselines in data-to-text generation tasks, particularly in zero-shot settings.
Data2Text Studio improves interactivity and interpretability of generated text through automated text generation from structured data.
An end-to-end trained two-step generation model will significantly outperform competitive comparisons in generating fluent and correct text.
Current neural generation methods fail to convincingly approximate human-generated documents in data-to-text generation tasks.
The Plan-then-Generate framework will improve the controllability of neural data-to-text models, enhancing both the generation quality and output diversity.
Text-to-text pre-training in the form of T5 enables simple, end-to-end transformer based models to outperform pipelined neural architectures tailored for data-to-text generation.
Prototype-to-Generate (P2G) framework significantly improves model performance for table-to-text generation under few-shot scenario.
A template-based input representation considerably improves the model’s generalization capability in data-to-text generation.
Training models on contrastive examples reduces errors in data-to-text generation.
Data augmentation for text generation will improve the quality of generated text when finetuning GPT-2 on low-data regimes.
Learning a policy that directly manipulates segments of neighbor text allows for more interpretable and controllable generation in data-to-text generation tasks.
CycleGT is an effective approach to overcome the data scarcity problem in the fields of graph-to-text and text-to-graph.

TABT5 generates natural language text based on tables and textual inputs and achieves new state-of-the-art results on several domains, including spreadsheet formula prediction with a 15% increase in sequence accuracy, QA with a 2.5% increase in sequence accuracy and data-to-text generation with a 2.5% increase in BLEU.
The enhanced transformer model will significantly improve content correctness and performance in data-to-text generation tasks compared to current state-of-the-art systems.
DiffuSeq achieves comparable or even better performance than six established baselines for sequence-to-sequence text generation tasks.
The proposed Transformer-based generation framework will achieve faithful neural table-to-text generation by enforcing content-matching constraints.
Combining delexicalisation and copying yields the strongest improvement in handling rare items in data-to-text generation.
DART poses new challenges to existing data-to-text datasets and facilitates out-of-domain generalization.
Operation-guided Attention-based sequence-to-sequence network (OpAtt) improves the fidelity of neural data-to-text generation to the input structured data.
The proposed model outperforms all comparison methods on both fully-supervised and few-shot settings for knowledge graph-to-text generation.
Leveraging richer training signals will improve the preservation of structural information in graph-to-text generation models.
The proposed method of generating text in a progressive manner significantly improves upon the fine-tuned GPT-2 in terms of domain-specific quality and sample efficiency.
SeqDiffuSeq will improve generation quality and inference time for sequence-to-sequence text generation compared to existing methods.
Pretrained Logical Form Generator (PLOG) framework will improve generation fidelity in logical table-to-text generation tasks.
Pre-trained language models (PLMs) can generalize from a few task examples, but their efficacy at data-to-text generation (D2T) is largely unexplored.
A search-and-learning approach that leverages pretrained language models will improve semantic coverage in few-shot data-to-text generation.
Incorporating auxiliary entity information in training will improve generation fidelity in open domain table-to-text generation.
The ToTTo dataset can serve as a useful research benchmark for high-precision conditional text generation.
Retrieval-augmented text generation has achieved state-of-the-art performance in many NLP tasks.

The equivariance learning framework will improve table-to-text generation performance by effectively expressing the relations of content pieces in the table and making the model robust to content-invariant structural transformations.
The neural graph-to-sequence model will show superior results compared to existing methods in the literature for AMR-to-text generation.
Restoring information balance and revamping the task to focus on fact-grounded data-to-text generation will improve data fidelity over state-of-the-art models.
Decoupled content selection in text generation allows for explicit control over the content of generated text.
DisCup will achieve a new state-of-the-art control performance while maintaining efficient and high-quality text generation.
Graph convolutional networks will outperform standard sequence-to-sequence methods in generating text from graph-structured data.
Retrieval-augmented text generation has remarkable advantages and has achieved state-of-the-art performance in many NLP tasks.
The proposed method for multi-aspect controllable text generation outperforms several strong baselines on attribute relevance and text quality.

given_idea: Soft prompt learning framework with the Multilingual Verbalizer will achieve state-of-the-art performance in cross-lingual natural language inference compared to previous methods.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Discrete and soft prompting perform better than finetuning in multilingual cases: Crosslingual transfer and in-language training of multilingual natural language inference.
A novel prompt-learning based framework will enhance cross-lingual natural language inference by reformulating the problem to a masked language modeling problem using cross-lingual templates.
Incorporating external knowledge into the verbalizer will improve and stabilize prompt-tuning for text classification.
The unified prompt will significantly outperform the strong baselines across different languages.
Learning prompts by gradient descent will significantly improve the performance of language models across multiple tasks.
Prompt transfer is promising for improving prompt tuning in natural language processing.
PromptSource addresses the emergent challenges in using natural language prompts to train and query language models.
The performance of a prompt is coupled with the extent to which the model is familiar with the language it contains.
Prompt-tuning achieves much better cross-lingual transfer than fine-tuning across datasets.
The black-box prompt tuning framework for vision-language models outperforms both hand-crafted prompt engineering and gradient-based prompt learning methods.
Co-training improves the performance of prompt-based learning by using unlabeled data.

An efficient method for retrieving prompts for in-context learning using annotated data and a language model will substantially outperform prior work and multiple baselines across sequence-to-sequence tasks.
RLPrompt optimizes discrete prompts for language models using reinforcement learning, enhancing training efficiency and performance across various tasks.

Soft Prompt Transfer significantly boosts the performance of Prompt Tuning across many tasks and matches or outperforms standard Model Tuning on the SuperGLUE benchmark while using up to 27,000× fewer task-specific parameters.
The ASK ME ANYTHING prompting method will improve the performance of large language models by aggregating multiple effective prompts compared to traditional single prompt methods.
The proposed multilingual speech recognition system outperforms both individual monolingual systems and systems built with a multi-task learning approach.
Prompt tuning can overcome catastrophic forgetting to enable zero-shot cross-lingual generation.
An iterative context-aware prompter will effectively elicit relevant knowledge from pre-trained language models for multi-step inference.
Prompt-based Data Augmentation model (PromDA) will boost the performance of Natural Language Understanding models compared to competitive baseline models.
FewVLM with prompt-based learning outperforms Frozen which is 31x larger than FewVLM by 18.2% point and achieves comparable results to a 246x larger model, PICa.
Dual context-guided continuous prompt tuning method will outperform existing prompt tuning methods in few-shot learning tasks.
Progressive Prompts will significantly improve average test accuracy in continual learning for language models compared to prior methods.
Developing and refining prompt-based strategies will significantly enhance the performance of large language models for clinical named entity recognition tasks.
Continued pretraining that incorporates a trainable prompt during multi-task learning leads to improved promptability in both zero- and few-shot settings compared to existing methods.
Automatically discovering better prompts can improve the accuracy of estimating the knowledge contained in language models from 31.1% to 39.6%.
Contrastive demonstration tuning will improve the performance of prompt-tuning in pre-trained language models compared to existing methods.
Prompt-based Continued Pre-training (PCP) consistently improves the performance of state-of-the-art prompt-based fine-tuning approaches in both semi-supervised and fully-supervised settings.
Language Model Programming (LMP) generalizes language model prompting from pure text prompts to an intuitive combination of text prompting and scripting.
'This paper provides a comprehensive survey of cutting-edge research on reasoning with language model prompting.'
Prototypical verbalizer (ProtoVerb) significantly outperforms current automatic verbalizers, especially when training data is extremely scarce.
Soft Context Sharing for Prompt Tuning will significantly outperform single-task prompt tuning methods for vision-language models on few-shot image recognition tasks.
Black-box Discrete Prompt Learning will efficiently adapt pre-trained language models across different downstream tasks without fine-tuning the model in the cloud.
Prompt-aligned Gradient will demonstrate stronger few-shot generalization ability compared to state-of-the-art prompt tuning methods.
Instance-aware prompt learning method learns a different prompt for each instance and achieves state-of-the-art results on the SuperGLUE few-shot learning benchmark.
Prompt distribution learning will effectively adapt a pre-trained vision-language model to downstream recognition tasks by learning low-bias prompts and capturing the distribution of diverse prompts.
Hierarchy-aware Prompt Tuning method will achieve state-of-the-art performances on hierarchical text classification datasets.
Prompt-based learning allows the language model to perform few-shot or zero-shot learning, adapting to new scenarios with few or no labeled data.
PromptIDE allows users to experiment with prompt variations, visualize prompt performance, and iteratively optimize prompts.
Unified Prompt Tuning (UPT) framework will lead to better few-shot text classification for BERT-style models by explicitly capturing prompting semantics from non-target NLP datasets.
Cross-lingual pretraining significantly outperforms monolingual pretraining in speech recognition tasks.
Synthetic prompting will improve the quality of reasoning demonstrations for large language models compared to existing prompting techniques.

Properly optimized prompt tuning can be universally effective across a wide range of model scales and natural language understanding tasks, matching the performance of fine-tuning while having only 0.1%-3% tuned parameters.
Prompt-based few-shot learning in dialogue tasks will achieve competitive performance to fully trained state-of-the-art models.

Prompts Augmented by Retrieval Crosslingually (PARC) pipeline improves the zero-shot performance on low-resource languages by augmenting the context with semantically similar sentences retrieved from a high-resource language.
Prompt distribution learning will significantly improve the performance of a pre-trained vision-language model on downstream recognition tasks compared to existing methods.

Automatic Prompt Engineer (APE) will generate instructions that outperform prior large language model baselines and achieve comparable performance to human-generated instructions across multiple NLP tasks.
Decomposed Prompting allows it to outperform prior work on few-shot prompting using Large Language Models.
Multilingual multilayer perceptron improves automatic speech recognition performance across language families.
Adding a new language using active learning to the EuroParl corpus provides a significant improvement compared to a random sentence selection baseline.
Maieutic Prompting achieves up to 20% better accuracy than state-of-the-art prompting methods and improves robustness in inference while providing interpretable rationales.

given_idea: ProToCo will significantly improve the factuality assessment capability of pre-trained language models in few-shot and zero-shot fact verification tasks compared to existing methods.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: FactPEGASUS substantially improves factuality evaluated by multiple automatic metrics and humans compared to the original pre-training objective.
Continuously pre-trained language models achieve better robustness in factual knowledge capturing compared to traditional methods.
The zero-shot framework will outperform fully-supervised approaches in correcting factual errors in textual knowledge bases.
Utilizing the perplexity score in few-shot learning for fact-checking can outperform the Major Class baseline by more than an absolute 10% on the F1-Macro metric across multiple datasets.
Generating scientific claims from scientific sentences will improve zero-shot fact checking for biomedical claims.
The compositional approach is more effective than the machine learning method in recognizing the factuality values of predications.
FactChecker will yield higher accuracy than existing approaches for truth-finding by leveraging linguistic features to infer believability of fact candidates.
The two-step approach for event factuality identification achieves a higher performance than that of the state-of-the-art rule-based system.
The evaluation results show sizable improvements over the baseline in automating the fact-checking process for claims about images.
Changing the popularity, transparency, homogeneity, and functionality properties of the facts in an experiment significantly influences the performance of fact checking algorithms.
Language models will serve as effective end-to-end fact checkers without the need for external knowledge or explicit retrieval components.
ExFaKT framework significantly increases the recall of fact-spotting while preserving high precision and effectively helps humans perform fact-checking.
The novel model for factuality prediction will outperform previous methods on all three datasets.
The classifier achieves an F1 score of 0.76 to detect the false class and other fact check articles.
End-to-end multimodal fact-checking and explanation generation will improve the assessment of claim truthfulness compared to existing methods.
Improving the quality of fact-checking requires systematic changes in the civic, informational, and technological contexts.
GO FIGURE provides a robust and efficient evaluation of factuality metrics that is extensible to multiple types of factual consistency and standard generation metrics.
Incorporating prior knowledge into fact-finding algorithms will reduce error and allow the system to determine truth respective to the user rather than the majority.
Detecting previously fact-checked claims can save time and effort in manual fact-checking processes.
Pre-trained Language Models (PLMs) capture factual knowledge ineffectively because of depending on inadequate associations.
Current natural language processing fact-checking cannot realistically combat real-world misinformation because it depends on unrealistic assumptions about counter-evidence in the data.
FactGraph will improve factual consistency in summarization compared to previous approaches.
A weakly-supervised, model-based approach for verifying factual consistency will substantially outperform previous models in assessing the factual consistency of abstractive text summarization.
The factual consistency of large language models is measured by comparing scores assigned to factually consistent versus factually inconsistent summaries.
WikiFactCheck-English dataset will allow the development of fact checking systems that can better process claims and evidence in the real world.
Intelligent technologies can support human fact-checkers in various steps of the fact-checking process.
Developing fact checking systems is likely to be of use to various members of society.
Automated fact-checking has gained increasing attention due to the growth of online false information.
A novel, unsupervised network-flow based approach will effectively discern true statements from false ones in computational fact checking.
Modeling the extracted justification in conjunction with the claim provides a significant improvement in fact-checking tasks regardless of the machine learning model used.
Automated fact checking using natural language processing works well in some cases, though generalized fact checking still needs improvement prior to widespread use.
Factual ablation can be used to automatically measure factual consistency in content transfer by assessing the model's preference for more factually relevant grounding documents.
FactKG can advance both reliability and practicality in knowledge graph-based fact verification.
Decomposing a complex claim into a comprehensive set of yes-no subquestions whose answers influence the veracity of the claim will improve the effectiveness of fact-checking.
The objectionable methodological practices in fact checking undermine the naïve political epistemology at work in journalism.
PASTA will achieve state-of-the-art performance on table-based fact verification datasets compared to previous methods.
Increased abstractiveness generally leads to a drop in factuality, but the rate of factuality decay depends on factors such as the data that the system was trained on.
LogicalFactChecker leverages logical operations for fact checking and achieves state-of-the-art performance on TABFACT.
Two neural models for event factuality prediction yield significant performance gains over previous models on three event factuality datasets.
A general-purpose framework for fully-automatic fact checking using external sources will effectively distinguish false claims from factually true claims.
Jointly modeling sentence extraction and verification on the FEVER shared task will improve the accuracy of fact checking compared to separate models.
A linguistic-oriented computational model will improve the understanding of event factuality in natural language.

Existing fact-checking models trained on non-dialogue data fail to perform well on fact-checking in dialogue, and a simple yet data-efficient solution can effectively improve fact-checking performance in this context.
Using word embeddings to classify misinformation will enhance the efficiency of the fact-checking process by identifying similar content.
Reformulating social-media input improves the performance of various fact-checking models compared to checking the tweet text in its entirety.
A two-staged sentence selection strategy will enhance evidence retrieval in the Fact Extraction and Verification challenge.
The task asks to predict which claims in a political debate should be prioritized for fact-checking.
The dataset is a challenging benchmark for the evaluation of multilingual fact-checking models.
Existing fact checking systems that perform well on claims in formal style significantly degenerate on colloquial claims with the same semantics.
Sampling negative sentences is important for improving the performance and decreasing the computational complexity in the fact extraction and verification task.

given_idea: The combination of a self-contrastive siamese network and the proposed neural Bregman network outperforms the baselines in two linear classification settings on three long document topic classification tasks.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Hierarchical attention network for document classification will outperform previous methods by a substantial margin.
An effective convolutional attention network will improve multi-label document classification performance on clinical documents compared to prior models.
A hierarchical model with self attention for topic spotting will show superior performance over previously proposed techniques for topic spotting and deep models for text classification.

The Label-Indexed Neural Topic Model (LI-NTM) outperforms existing neural topic models in document reconstruction benchmarks, particularly in low labeled data regimes and for datasets with informative labels.

The neural topic model via optimal transport will significantly outperform state-of-the-art neural topic models in discovering coherent and diverse topics and deriving better document representations for both regular and short texts.
Deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) provide a superior representation strategy for document image classification and retrieval compared to handcrafted alternatives.
Layer-Assisted Neural Topic Model (LANTM) significantly outperforms existing models on topic quality, text classification, and link prediction.
The proposed framework outperforms other state-of-the-art neural topic models in terms of topic coherence across various domains, vocabulary sizes, and document lengths.
SelfExplain facilitates interpretability without sacrificing performance in neural text classifiers.
Message Passing Attention network for Document understanding (MPAD) will achieve competitive performance on text classification tasks compared to state-of-the-art methods.
The proposed framework with a two-branch Siamese Network significantly outperforms other state-of-the-art unsupervised methods on semantic textual similarity and classification tasks.

Neural topic models are better in uncovering cohesive topics and representing input documents for downstream classification, while traditional probabilistic models perform better in modeling the input documents.
Projection attention neural network PRADO will achieve near state of the art performance on multiple long document classification tasks while maintaining a compact size.
Deep pyramid convolutional neural networks will outperform previous best models on benchmark datasets for sentiment classification and topic categorization.
TopicNet will outperform related deep topic models on discovering deeper interpretable topics and mining better document representations.
The self-attention based document interaction network satisfies the permutation-equivariant requirement and can generate scores for document sets of varying sizes.
Sawtooth factorial topic embedding guided gamma belief network will outperform other neural topic models on extracting deeper interpretable topics and deriving better document representations.
SoPa is comparable or better than both a BiLSTM baseline and a CNN baseline on three text classification tasks.
The proposed approach will maintain the strengths of Recurrent Neural Networks and Convolutional Neural Networks for text classification.
Contrastive learning based hybrid networks will improve classification accuracy in long-tailed image classification compared to existing methods.

Hierarchical Attention-based Recurrent Neural Network (HARNN) will effectively classify documents into the most relevant categories level by level while capturing the associations between texts and the hierarchical structure.
The strong performance of convolutional networks and self-attentional networks could also be due to their ability to extract semantic features from the source text.
Disconnected recurrent neural network (DRNN) achieves better performance on text categorization compared to recurrent neural network (RNN) and convolutional neural network (CNN) models.
Initializing convolutional filters with semantic features will improve the performance of convolutional neural networks for text classification tasks.
Enhancing a segmenter based on a hierarchical attention BiLSTM network will improve context modeling in neural topic segmentation.
ContrastNet will effectively address the discriminative representation and overfitting problems in few-shot text classification.
Deep Bregman divergence will enhance contrastive loss in self-supervised learning, improving the quality of learned representations compared to conventional methods.
The Topic Attention Model (TAM) significantly improves supervised learning tasks, including classification and regression, and achieves lower perplexity for document modeling.
The logit-normal posterior modification of the Neural Variational Document Model will achieve better alignment to user-desired topics compared to other Neural Topic Models.
Hierarchical Deep Learning for Text classification (HDLTex) will improve document classification performance compared to traditional supervised classifiers in large document collections.
Continuous Self-Attention models with neural ODE networks (CSAODE) will improve parameter efficiency and outperform state-of-the-art models on text classification tasks.
Neural Vector Space Model (NVSM) performs better at document ranking than existing latent semantic vector space methods.
Character-level convolutional networks could achieve state-of-the-art or competitive results for text classification.
The semantic-unit-based dilated convolution model will demonstrate significant advantages over baseline models in multi-label text classification tasks.
C-LSTM neural network will outperform both convolutional neural network and long short-term memory recurrent neural network in text classification tasks.
Combining contextualized representations with neural topic models produces more meaningful and coherent topics than traditional bag-of-words topic models and recent neural models.
Very deep convolutional networks for text processing will improve performance on text classification tasks compared to existing architectures.
Investigating very deep neural network architectures and advanced training strategies will reduce the error in document image classification by more than half.
BIGRUs with label-wise attention perform better than other current state of the art methods for Large-Scale Multi-Label Text Classification.
A multi-task convolutional neural network with the Leaky Unit will filter feature flows between tasks, enhancing performance in text classification.
Hierarchical methods based on Probabilistic Label Trees outperform Label-Wise Attention Networks in large-scale multi-label text classification.
A multimodal approach to topic labelling using a simple feedforward neural network will outperform single-modality topic labelling systems.
The hierarchical convolutional document model will effectively identify and extract topic-relevant sentences from labelled documents.
Recent advances in neural networks will outperform traditional hand-engineered feature-based methods on the semantic level multi-class compound classification task for Sanskrit.
The siamese convolutional neural network-based system will achieve higher accuracy and lower relative error in off-topic response detection compared to the standard vector space model.

The hierarchical taxonomy-aware and attentional graph capsule recurrent CNNs framework will significantly improve the performance of large-scale multi-label text classification compared to state-of-the-art approaches.
Feature-based approaches perform better than neural network classifiers for text datasets of this size.

given_idea: Knowledge Injection into Language Models (KILM) enables models to retain more knowledge and hallucinate less while preserving their original performance on general Natural Language Understanding and Natural Language Generation tasks.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Integrating knowledge into pretrained language models will improve their knowledge awareness and performance on natural language processing tasks.

Knowledge Infused Decoding (KID) will outperform many task-optimized state-of-the-art models in knowledge-intensive natural language generation tasks.
The knowledge graph language model (KGLM) achieves significantly better performance than a strong baseline language model.
The pretrained knowledge-based multilingual language models (KMLMs) demonstrate significant performance improvements on a wide range of knowledge-intensive cross-lingual tasks.

Decomposable knowledge-enhanced pre-trained language model (DKPLM) outperforms other knowledge-enhanced pre-trained language models significantly over zero-shot knowledge probing tasks and multiple knowledge-aware language understanding tasks while achieving higher inference speed.
Using external knowledge graphs to store knowledge will improve the parameter efficiency and performance of natural language understanding models on downstream tasks.

The concept-aware language model (CALM) can pack more commonsense knowledge into the parameters of a pre-trained text-to-text transformer without relying on external knowledge graphs, yielding better performance on both natural language understanding and natural language generation tasks.

Finetuning is mainly about exposing knowledge induced by language-modeling training, rather than learning new task-specific linguistic knowledge.

KEPLER will better integrate factual knowledge into pre-trained language models and produce effective text-enhanced knowledge embedding.
Retrieval augmented language models can learn knowledge intensive tasks with very few training examples, outperforming large models with significantly fewer parameters.

Pretrained language models can induce natural language rules from natural language facts.

Incorporating knowledge graph entities and fine-grained relationships into the language learning process will significantly enhance the performance of pretrained language models.
CoCoLM will help pre-trained language models better incorporate complex commonsense knowledge.

Learning from natural language feedback will improve the performance of language models in generating task outputs.

The neuro-symbolic approach for dynamically constructing knowledge graphs will achieve significant performance boosts over pretrained language models and vanilla knowledge models for zero-shot commonsense question answering.
KnowledgeEditor can edit factual knowledge in language models without the need for expensive re-training or fine-tuning.
Commonsense knowledge models can rapidly adapt from limited examples, indicating that knowledge graph fine-tuning serves to learn an interface to encoded knowledge learned during pretraining.

Large pretrained language models require a much larger quantity of data to acquire commonsense knowledge and skills necessary for mastering typical downstream natural language understanding tasks.
Self-evolution learning for pretrained language models will effectively extract knowledge from input pretraining data and improve performance on downstream tasks.

The integration of natural language understanding into reinforcement learning will enhance the ability of models to acquire world knowledge and improve decision-making in real-world tasks.

Research in natural language generation can be adapted to dialog systems, and machine learning techniques can overcome the high cost of hand-crafting knowledge-based generation systems.
