A statistical method will uncover the underlying latent semantic structure in dense word embeddings.
A deep neural network diachronic distributional model will effectively capture the semantic trajectory of a word over time.
Exploiting distributional similarity information during training can improve visual word predictors for certain words, such as entry-level nouns or hypernyms.

The hierarchical generative probabilistic model incorporating both n-gram statistics and latent topic variables exhibits better predictive accuracy than either a hierarchical Dirichlet bigram language model or a unigram topic model.
Separating syntactic from semantic learning may be a useful heuristic for capturing compositional structure.
Introducing suitable structures into the conditional probability distributions will improve the prediction of new words in stochastic language modelling.
The tensor-based factorization model for semantic compositionality exceeds the state of the art in a similarity task for transitive phrases.
Adding gold-standard bracketing within each noun phrase in the Penn Treebank improves the ability to learn the correct internal structure of noun phrases.
Distributional models can distinguish between semantic relations, and distributional similarity indicates property overlap.
Incorporating additional lexical n-gram probabilities into the baseline model eliminates all differences in accuracy between unlexicalized sequential and hierarchical models.
A parameterized compositional switch will adaptively determine whether the meaning of a phrase should be composed of its two constituents.
The novel unsupervised approach to detecting the compositionality of multi-word expressions will achieve state-of-the-art performance on a verb-object dataset of human compositionality ratings.
Constructing interpretable word vectors from hand-crafted linguistic resources will yield competitive performance compared to standard distributional approaches.
Modeling metaphor as linear transformations in a vector space improves semantic representations in compositional distributional semantic models.
The proposed method (CNNSE) allows word and phrase vectors to adapt to the notion of composition and provides an interpretable vector space model for semantics.
Anchored packed dependency trees have the potential to capture the full sentential contexts of a lexeme and provide a uniform basis for the composition of distributional knowledge.
Using information contained in the dictionary as additional training data improves accuracy in learning new nouns.
The wider context in which a sentence is located can contribute to a distributional representation of sentence meaning.
The proposed model for learning a region representation for word meaning in semantic space can predict, with high precision, when a hyponymy-based inference rule is applicable.
Distributional semantics will foster greater cross-fertilization of theoretical and computational approaches to language.
A novel taxonomy of relations will improve the automatic interpretation of noun compounds in natural language processing applications.

given_idea: ConvSumX is more faithful towards input text compared to existing cross-lingual summarization corpora.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: The proposed method can significantly outperform the baselines in cross-lingual summarization tasks.

The robust neural abstractive summarization system for cross-lingual summarization achieves significantly higher fluency than a standard copy-attention summarizer on automatically translated input documents.
MultiSumm model will significantly outperform a multi-lingual baseline model in multilingual abstractive summarization.
This survey presents the first systematic critical review on the datasets, approaches, and challenges in cross-lingual summarization.
The proposed cross-lingual summarisation corpus will facilitate the analysis of cross-lingual summarisation tasks and improve the performance of multi-lingual pre-trained models.
The multilingual dataset will enable new research directions for the text summarization community.

Neural Cross-Lingual Summarization (NCLS) achieves remarkable improvement over traditional pipeline methods on both English-to-Chinese and Chinese-to-English cross-lingual summarization human-corrected test sets.
The ConvSumX Challenge will motivate researchers to investigate solutions that integrate conversation summarization and machine translation.
CTSUM will produce summaries with better content quality and higher certainty compared to existing summarization systems.
The proposed method for direct cross-lingual summarization significantly outperforms the baseline approaches while being more cost efficient during inference.
Different approaches of constructing cross-lingual summarization datasets will lead to different degrees of translationese.
The proposed system SciSumm performs better in content selection than the existing summarization system MEAD.
Mixed-lingual pre-training will effectively boost the performance of cross-lingual summarization.
Considering the translation quality of each sentence in the English-to-Chinese cross-language summarization process will improve the quality of the cross-language summary.
Jointly learning to align and summarize will ease the cross-lingual summarization training and outperform competitive models.
MEAD is a comprehensive, public domain, open source, multidocument multilingual summarization environment that has been used in a variety of summarization applications.
Using bilingual information from both the source and translated documents will improve cross-language document summarization.
A hierarchical model for cross-lingual summarization will generate better summaries than comparison models in the few-shot setting.
The new hMDS corpus for multi-document summarization is significantly different from homogeneous corpora and poses new challenges in the field.
The DACSA corpus can be used to train summarization models for Catalan and Spanish.
The CNN-corpus allows quantitative and qualitative assessments of extractive summarization strategies.
The comparative summarization framework CoCoSum can produce higher-quality contrastive and common summaries than state-of-the-art opinion summarization models.
The Multi-Task framework for Cross-Lingual Abstractive Summarization (MCLAS) significantly outperforms three baseline models in both low-resource and full-dataset scenarios.
The contrastive aligned joint learning model for multilingual summarization (CALMS) achieves significant improvement over monolingual models in all languages.
This paper gives a comparative study of various text summarization techniques.
AREDSUM-CTX achieves significantly better performance than AREDSUM-SEQ and state-of-the-art extractive summarization baselines by modeling diversity explicitly in a separate step.
The multi-lingual dialogue summarization dataset MSAMSum will facilitate benchmarking and improve the understanding of dialogue summarization across six languages.
The proposed abstractive cross-language summarization framework outperforms the state-of-the-art extractive systems in both automatic and manual evaluations.
Distributed representations outperform ROUGE in recent corpora for abstractive news summarization.
The modified summarization salience protocol, Atomic Content Units (ACUs), will allow for a high inter-annotator agreement in summarization evaluation.
TVSum will produce superior quality summaries compared to several recently proposed approaches for video summarization.
XL-Sum induces competitive results compared to the ones obtained using similar monolingual datasets.
The hierarchical cross-modality semantic correlation learning model (HCSCL) significantly outperforms baseline methods in automatic summarization metrics and fine-grained diversity tests.
Z-Code++ will create a new state-of-the-art on 9 of 13 text summarization tasks across 5 languages.
State-of-the-art summarization models perform poorly on the Multi-LexSum task despite high-quality summaries in the training data.
The multilingual version of the Columbia Newsblaster news summarization system automatically collects, organizes, and summarizes news in multiple source languages.
This paper focuses on the survey of new summarization tasks and approaches in real-world applications.
A cascade architecture for neural text summarization will perform comparably to or better than end-to-end systems in terms of content selection and coherence.
The proposed model demonstrates superiority in multi-modal supplementary and complementary summarization compared to existing techniques.
Automatically generated summaries are ranked significantly higher than human-selected extracts on coherence and usability criteria.
SMART metric outperforms all competing metrics on the SummEval summarization meta-evaluation dataset.
The hybrid generation approach will lead to more faithful, relevant, and aggregation-sensitive summarization compared to conventional methods.
ViT5 significantly outperforms existing models and achieves state-of-the-art results on Vietnamese Text Summarization.
Highlight-based Reference-less Evaluation of Summarization (HighRES) improves inter-annotator agreement in comparison to using the source document directly.
Evolutionary Timeline Summarization (ETS) will effectively generate summaries that balance relevance, coverage, coherence, and cross-date diversity compared to traditional summarization methods.
The joint summarization approach outperforms other baselines and methods using videos or images only.
Automatic multi-modal summarization (MMS) is essential for obtaining key information from multiple forms of representations.
Multi-level Summarizer (MLS) outperforms strong baselines by up to 14.70% in the METEOR score.
The proposed approach for producing readable and cohesive citation-based summaries outperforms several baselines in terms of both extraction quality and fluency.
The structured convolutional decoder will generate summaries with better content coverage compared to existing sequential decoders.

given_idea: Self-Contrastive Learning (SSCL) will alleviate the over-smoothing problem in pre-trained language models, leading to improved unsupervised sentence representations.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Deep Contrastive Learning for Unsupervised Textual Representations will close the performance gap between unsupervised and supervised pretraining for universal sentence encoders.
Contrastive self-supervised learning at the sentence level will improve language understanding compared to existing token-based pretraining approaches.
Virtual augmentation Supported Contrastive Learning of sentence representations (VaSCL) sets a new state-of-the-art for unsupervised sentence representation learning.
Smoothed contrastive sentence embedding (IS-CSE) will improve generalization performance in unsupervised sentence embeddings compared to instance-level embeddings.
A novel generative self-supervised learning objective based on phrase reconstruction will achieve better performance in sentence representation learning compared to current contrastive methods.
Contrastive learning with Prompt-derived Virtual semantic Prototypes will improve unsupervised sentence embeddings compared to existing methods.
Multilingual contrastive pretraining significantly enhances sentence representations, yielding a large performance gain on both benchmarks.
Contrastive learning for unsupervised sentence embedding with soft negative samples (SNCSE) can obtain state-of-the-art performance on semantic textual similarity task.
Pairwise supervised contrastive learning of sentence representations will outperform previous state-of-the-art methods in understanding sentence semantics.
Self-guided contrastive learning method will improve the quality of BERT sentence representations compared to competitive baselines.
The information minimization based contrastive learning model InforMin-CL will retain useful information and discard redundant information in unsupervised sentence representation learning.
The self-training technique for language model fine-tuning will outperform other state-of-the-art supervised and semi-supervised counterparts on sentence classification tasks.
Weakly supervised contrastive learning framework (WCL) improves the quality of self-supervised representations across different datasets.
Debiased Contrastive Learning of unsupervised sentence Representations will alleviate the influence of improper negatives and improve the uniformity of the representation space.
Contrastive learning with Augmented and Retrieved Data for Sentence embedding (CARDS) significantly surpasses the current state-of-the-art on STS benchmarks in the unsupervised setting.
Using unsupervised word representations as extra word features will improve the accuracy of supervised natural language processing systems.
Contrastive self-supervised training objectives will improve language modeling, zero to few-shot learning, and pretraining data-efficiency in natural language processing.

Contrastive Learning for Sentence Representation (CLEAR) employs multiple sentence-level augmentation strategies to learn a noise-invariant sentence representation and outperforms existing methods on SentEval and GLUE benchmarks.
Pretrained language models implicitly learn sentence representations that cluster by domains without supervision.
MirrorWiC improves word-in-context representations in pretrained language models through a fully unsupervised approach.
Deep Bregman divergence will enhance contrastive loss in self-supervised learning, improving the quality of learned representations compared to conventional methods.
Mean-max attention autoencoder will outperform state-of-the-art unsupervised single methods in learning universal sentence representations.
Supervised contrastive learning objective for fine-tuning will improve generalization and robustness of pre-trained language models compared to cross-entropy loss.

The proposed framework for learning sentence representations will outperform state-of-the-art unsupervised and supervised representation learning methods on several downstream natural language processing tasks while achieving an order of magnitude speedup in training time.
Peer-Contrastive Learning (PCL) with diverse augmentations will improve the quality of unsupervised sentence embeddings compared to existing mono-augmenting strategies.
Contrastive pre-training on unsupervised data at scale leads to high quality vector representations of text and code.
PromptBERT improves BERT sentence embeddings through a novel contrastive learning method and a new unsupervised training objective.
Hard negative examples are essential for maintaining strong gradient signals in the training process for unsupervised sentence representation learning.
Pair-level Supervised Contrastive Learning approach (PairSCL) will outperform existing methods in natural language inference tasks by improving accuracy on sentence pair representations.
Self-training a self-attentive model sets a new state-of-the-art for disfluency detection, providing benefits orthogonal to pre-trained contextualized word representations.
Prototypical Contrastive Learning (PCL) achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple unsupervised representation learning benchmarks, with >10% accuracy improvement in low-resource transfer tasks.

The contrastive self-training framework, COSINE, will enable fine-tuning language models with weak supervision, outperforming the strongest baseline and achieving competitive performance with fully-supervised fine-tuning methods.
Prompt-based Contrastive Learning for Sentence Embeddings will improve performance in domain shift settings and enhance pairwise discriminative power compared to existing contrastive methods.
Contrastive learning improves performance in various natural language processing tasks and provides characteristics such as task-agnostic sentence representation and data-efficient learning.
Convolutional neural networks trained on pre-trained word vectors will achieve excellent results on sentence-level classification tasks.
The multimodal contrastive learning approach will improve the performance of sentence embeddings on semantic textual similarity tasks.

Universal sentence representations trained using supervised data from the Stanford Natural Language Inference datasets can consistently outperform unsupervised methods like SkipThought vectors on a wide range of transfer tasks.
SimCSE will advance the state-of-the-art sentence embeddings through a simple contrastive learning framework.
The unsupervised approach to sentence boundary detection achieves good results without any further amendments or language-specific resources.
Self-supervised contrastive representation learning will improve the performance of spoken question answering tasks.
Self-Contrastive Decorrelation (SCD) achieves comparable results with state-of-the-art methods on multiple benchmarks without using contrastive pairs.
Self-training improves pretraining for natural language understanding.
Differentiable data augmentation during contrastive learning will yield significant improvements over existing methods for sentence representation learning.
Pretraining with Contrastive Sentence Objectives improves discourse performance of language models.
Language modeling is an effective pretraining task for sentence understanding, but the paradigm of pretraining and freezing sentence encoders may not be ideal for further work.
The contrastive learning framework CLRCMD enhances the quality of sentence similarity and their interpretation compared to existing methods.
The proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art unsupervised models on most benchmark tasks, highlighting the robustness of the produced general-purpose sentence embeddings.
Pretrained language models can generate high-quality sentence embeddings without the need for labeled data, finetuning, or modifications to the pretraining objective.

The composite baseline made of self-supervised contrastive representation learning, clustering, and language modeling shows better than chance performance on all four metrics for spoken language modeling from raw speech.
Cloze-driven pretraining of self-attention networks will provide significant performance gains across a variety of language understanding problems.

given_idea: A bias affects human naming choices for people playing sports, with speakers producing names indicating the sport more often for men or boys than for women or girls.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Unisex names are given with substantial frequency to both genders, with a change in preference from males to females over the years in Pennsylvania.
The collective concept person/people is not gender-neutral but rather prioritizes men over women.
Named entity recognition models exhibit gender bias, as relatively more female names are not recognized as PERSON entities compared to male names.
Adapters introduce a new type of bias where words become more similar to female- than male pronouns.
Gender fair language would reduce the impact of the gender connotation on participants’ perceptions.

Stereotypical gender associations in language have decreased over time but still remain, with career and science terms demonstrating positive male gender bias and family and arts terms demonstrating negative male gender bias.
Gender bias in Natural Language Processing systems varies across different human languages.
A language-model-based approach will quantify differences in questions posed to female vs. male athletes in sports journalism.
Gender prejudice occurs more in gendered rather than genderless languages.
Social biases exist in large English debate portals, with debate corpora containing unbalanced and biased data, mostly in favor of male people with European-American names.
Gender is a social construct that varies across cultures.
The degree to which gender bias differs with the corpora used for training can significantly impact the bias learned by Natural Language Processing systems.
Adolescent girls are required to navigate gender constructs and sexuality stereotypes in traditionally masculine sports.
The level of concern is dependent on gender, with females being more likely to express concern than males.

Generation Z demonstrates an awareness of gender inequality in the media and in school sport, indicating that intercultural differences exist and highlighting knowledge gaps in higher education regarding gender equity in sport.
Biased data in Artificial Intelligence-Natural Language Processing models amplifies gender bias, resulting in the underrepresentation of the female gender in various fields.

The first-order approach to measuring gender bias of occupational words shows higher correlations with actual gender bias statistics of the U.S. job market compared to vector similarity-based approaches.
Gender-fair language will contribute to the reduction of gender stereotyping and discrimination.

Only peer interactions that are not driven by cultural beliefs about the general competence of men and women or interactions in which women are status- or power-advantaged over men are likely to undermine the gender system.
The assignment of genders to nouns is not entirely arbitrary but may to some extent reflect the perceived masculine or feminine properties of the nouns’ referents.
The proposed taxonomy facilitates the identification and mitigation of gender bias in English text.
Existing language generation models embed gender bias.
The majority of the articles do not make their theorization of gender explicit, even if they clearly define 'bias.'
Systematic gender bias exists in coreference resolution systems, as confirmed by the evaluation of Winogender schemas.
Wikipedia pages tend to intermingle personal life events with professional events for females but not for males.
Audio will provide additional information to reduce gender bias in speech translation.
Larger capacity models tend to exhibit more gender bias and greater stereotyping of occupations by gender.
Significant linguistic differences exist across individuals with different gender and stance in expressing opinions about abortion.
Using word association tests will provide a different perspective on gender stereotypes compared to word-embedding-based bias scores.
Sex bias against female participants in clinical studies persists, but results differ when studies vs participants are the measurement units.
A taxonomy is proposed for structural and contextual gender biases which can manifest themselves in text.
All tested models tend to over-rely on gender stereotypes when presented with natural inputs.
Gender bias in word embeddings from Wikipedia has developed over time, with art-related words becoming more female biased and career-related words more strongly associated with male than female.
There exists gender bias in the dataset and such bias is amplified by the embeddings.
Word pairs help to avoid a male bias in the gender-typing of professions and increase women's visibility; at the same time, they decrease the estimated salaries of typically feminine professions.
Grammatically masculine role-nouns in German favor a male-biased interpretation rather than a gender-neutral interpretation.

Gender stereotypes and the expectations they produce about both what women are like and how they should behave can result in devaluation of their performance, denial of credit to them for their successes, or their penalization for being competent.

A method for discouraging correlation between the predicted probability of an individual’s true occupation and a word embedding of their name will reduce race and gender biases without requiring access to protected attributes.
Gender-fair forms in French are an efficient tool for increasing the visibility of women, at least in nouns representing non-stereotypical activities.

A causal approach for mitigating gender bias in word vector relations will achieve state-of-the-art results on gender-debiasing tasks, lexical- and sentence-level evaluation tasks, and downstream coreference resolution tasks.
The gender of one conversation side influences lexical use of the other side.
The positivity bias applies to nouns and verbs as well as adjectives and is moderated by personality traits and gender.
Dialogue systems show significant prejudice towards different genders and races.

Respondents demonstrated implicit preference for White over Black and young over old, and stereotypic associations linking male terms with science and career and female terms with liberal arts and family.
This paper will present a history of women's involvement in sport prior to the federal legislation enacted to eliminate sexual discrimination in education and sport.
Gender is dictated by the general sound patterns of a language, and it goes beyond suffixes or word endings.
Identifying as a woman is associated with higher rates of support but also higher rates of disparagement.
Entrainment is more important to the perception of female-male social behavior than it is for same-gender pairs.
Including explicit gender indicators in semantic representations of online biographies impacts occupation classification and correlates with existing gender imbalances in occupations.
The criteria for membership in a gender category are significantly different in social versus (hetero)sexual circumstances.

given_idea: InfoMetIC achieves significantly better correlation with human judgements than existing metrics on multiple benchmarks.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Data-QuestEval metric obtains state-of-the-art correlations with human judgment on the WebNLG and WikiBio benchmarks.
The uniformly designed metrics achieve stronger or comparable correlations with human judgement compared to state-of-the-art metrics in each of diverse tasks.
LEPOR will yield state-of-the-art correlation with human judgments compared to classic metrics BLEU, TER, Meteor-1.3, AMBER, and MP4IBM1.

QuestEval substantially improves the correlation with human judgments over four evaluation dimensions (consistency, coherence, fluency, and relevance) compared to established metrics such as ROUGE or BERTScore.
The USL-H score achieves good correlations with human judgment and maintains its configurability towards different aspects and metrics.
The family of metrics derived from population estimation methods will show a higher correlation to human evaluation than existing metrics on several challenging tasks.
Flexible semi-automatic to automatic summary evaluation metrics will improve human-metric correlations compared to existing metrics.
Language Model Augmented Relevance Score (MARS) achieves higher correlation with human reference judgements compared to existing metrics.
The use of multiple references results in improved correlation between several automatic metrics and human judgement for both the quality and the diversity of system output.
New combinations of components outperform existing measures with respect to correlation to human ratings.
Perception Score achieves state-of-the-art results on all the tasks consistently in terms of correlation with human evaluation scores.
Automated metrics have stronger correlation with human judgments in the task-oriented setting compared to what has been observed in the non task-oriented setting.
RQUGE has a higher correlation with human judgment without relying on the reference question.
An embedding-based metric that has the highest correlation with human evaluations on a standard benchmark can have the lowest correlation if the amount of input noise or unknown tokens increases.
RankME significantly improves the reliability and consistency of human ratings compared to traditional evaluation methods.
The measure of rank correlation proposed in this paper is capable of being applied to both problems of comparing rankings.
The global algorithm achieves substantial improvements over previous work on both pairwise and rank correlation metrics.
Intrinsic metrics correlate significantly with human judgments of quality and understandability, but cannot replace human evaluation.
The FED metric will attain moderate to strong correlation with human judgement at both the turn and whole dialog levels.
The proposed evaluator achieves a strong correlation (> 0.6) with human judgement and generalizes robustly to diverse responses and corpora.
BlonDe achieves significantly higher Pearson’s r correlation with human judgments compared to previous metrics.
The rankings produced by metrics such as MaxMatch and I-measure do not correlate well with human judgments, while GLEU correlates much more closely with human judgments.
QAFactEval leads to a 14% average improvement over previous QA-based metrics on the SummaC factual consistency benchmark.
Word Embedding-Based automatic MT evaluation using Word Position Information (WE_WPI) achieves the highest correlation with human judgment among several representative metrics.
A novel metric to evaluate the factual consistency in text summarization via counterfactual estimation will improve the correlation with human judgments and the convenience of usage.
SUM-QE achieves very high correlations with human ratings, outperforming simpler models addressing these linguistic aspects.
The method obtains good results with respect to a reference ranking produced by human raters.
METEOR will show improved correlation with human judgments of translation quality compared to existing metrics.
UniEval will achieve a higher correlation with human judgments than existing metrics for multi-dimensional evaluation in Natural Language Generation.
The method has a relative improvement of at least 5.62% compared to the state-of-the-art on multiple metrics on the recommendation task.
The automatic evaluation metric Q^2 will show higher correlation with human judgments compared to other metrics for evaluating factual consistency in knowledge-grounded dialogue.
The proposed F1-based metric yields a changed system ranking compared to the original evaluation metric used in the Fake News Challenge Stage 1.
The correlation between the ranks computed by the SVM based ranking function and human ranking is significantly better than the correlation between ranking of individual features and human ranking.
Some popular relevance metrics do not pass sanity check criteria, and some metrics based on cosine similarity perform better than other metrics.
QRelScore will achieve a higher correlation with human judgments while being more robust to adversarial samples compared to existing metrics for question generation.
Collecting human judgments for high-scoring summaries will resolve the debate over which evaluation metrics to trust.

An unreferenced automated evaluation metric using large pre-trained language models will achieve higher correlation with human annotations in an online setting without requiring true responses for comparison during inference.
Current evaluation protocol is weakly correlated with human judgment and does not account for important characteristics such as factual correctness.
HCPC (Human Centered Product Comparison) significantly outperforms strong baselines in generating informative and factually consistent product comparison text.
The dataset significantly highlights the gap between the best model and human performance on various evaluation metrics.
TRUE will provide a comprehensive survey and assessment of factual consistency metrics that enables a more actionable and interpretable example-level meta-evaluation protocol.

The method of canonical correlation will maximize the strength of current automatic evaluations by finding the optimal linear combination of ROUGE scores to maximize correlation with human responsiveness.
Collecting human judgments allows for more nuanced comparisons of AMR generation systems compared to automated metrics.
BERTScore correlates better with human judgments and provides stronger model selection performance than existing metrics.
BLANC achieves a correlation with human evaluations comparable to the ROUGE family of summary quality measurements while allowing for fully human-free summary quality estimation.
The static attention based approach outperforms all the baselines on automatic and human evaluation.
The weighted correlation index will provide a more accurate measure of correlation in rankings with ties compared to Kendall's tau.
The proposed paradigm for evaluating image descriptions using human consensus captures human judgment of consensus better than existing metrics.
The benchmark datasets for EXplanaTion RAnking (EXTRA) will provide a standard way of evaluating recommendation explanations using ranking-oriented metrics.
Automated summary evaluation based on the BLEU method will provide encouraging correlations with human judgments despite the instability of manual evaluation.

given_idea: Memory-Augmented Recurrent Transformer (MART) generates more coherent and less repetitive paragraph captions than baseline methods, while maintaining relevance to the input video events.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Synchronized Deep Recurrent Attentive Writer (Sync-DRAW) can generate videos from simple captions and is efficient in learning the spatial and temporal information of the videos.
Hierarchical Recurrent Neural Encoder (HRNE) efficiently exploits video temporal structure in a longer range and outperforms state-of-the-art methods on video captioning benchmarks.
A novel end-to-end sequence-to-sequence model will generate captions for videos by associating a sequence of video frames to a sequence of words.
An end-to-end transformer model for dense video captioning will improve the consistency between event proposals and captioning during training.
Hierarchical Recurrent Neural Networks will significantly outperform current state-of-the-art methods in video captioning as demonstrated by BLEU@4 scores.
The LSTM–GAN system architecture will significantly outperform existing methods on standard public datasets for video captioning.
Temporal Generative Adversarial Networks conditioning on captions (TGANs-C) will generate plausible videos that match the given captions.
The multimodal Recurrent Neural Network (m-RNN) model outperforms state-of-the-art methods in generating image captions and retrieval tasks.
A hierarchical reinforcement learning framework for video captioning significantly outperforms all baseline methods on fine-grained video captioning.
Generating commonsense descriptions from videos will enhance video captioning by incorporating intentions, attributes, and effects.
Video Multiple Choice Caption (VideoMCC) is a new well-defined task with an easy-to-interpret performance measure for video comprehension.

The non-autoregressive decoding based model with a coarse-to-fine captioning procedure will alleviate the defects of slow inference speed and generic descriptions in mainstream video captioning methods.

HERO achieves new state of the art on multiple benchmarks over Text-based Video/Video-moment Retrieval, Video Question Answering, Video-and-language Inference and Video Captioning tasks across different domains.
The reconstruction network (RecNet) can boost the encoder-decoder models and leads to significant gains in video caption accuracy.
Applying sequence-level training with an integrated penalty on trigram repetition will produce more diverse paragraphs in image paragraph captioning.
M² - a Meshed Transformer with Memory for Image Captioning achieves a new state of the art in single-model and ensemble configurations on the 'Karpathy' test split and on the online test server.
The proposed framework for dense video captioning will show clear improvements compared to existing state-of-the-art techniques.
The proposed models produce captions that are more diverse and more accurate than a strong LSTM baseline or a 'vanilla' conditional variational auto-encoder with a fixed Gaussian prior.
Multi-modal Circulant Fusion (MCF) will achieve state-of-the-art performance for video captioning and improve performance in temporal activity localization via language (TALL) tasks.
Convolutional image captioning technique will demonstrate performance on par with the long-short-term-memory baseline while having a faster training time per number of parameters.
Bidirectional LSTM models achieve highly competitive performance to the state-of-the-art results on caption generation and significantly outperform recent methods on retrieval task.

The proposed video description model will achieve state-of-the-art performance on video description, video paragraph description, and image description while generating sentences that are better grounded in the video.
The proposed task of sequencing jumbled image-caption pairs will achieve strong results in forming a coherent story.
The proposed method outperforms the baselines in generating long captions for figure captioning.
EnTangled Attention enables the Transformer to exploit semantic and visual information simultaneously, achieving state-of-the-art performance on the MSCOCO image captioning dataset.
A model architecture based on Inception-ResNetv2 for image-feature extraction and Transformer for sequence modeling achieves the best performance when trained on the Conceptual Captions dataset.
Generating captions based on part-of-speech summaries will achieve high accuracy, faster computation, and high diversity compared to traditional beam search methods.
The Context-Aware Visual Policy network (CAVP) will improve sequence-level image captioning by effectively utilizing previous visual attentions as context for word generation.
Using BERT to encode complex semantics from video clips will significantly improve performance in video question answering compared to previous methods.
The end-to-end model generates captions for images embedded in news articles and outperforms the previous state of the art by a factor of four in CIDEr score.
Positional Self-Attention with Coattention (PSAC) significantly outperforms recurrent neural networks (RNNs) in video question answering tasks while requiring less computation time.
Tensor Product Generation Network (TPGN) outperforms LSTM based state-of-the-art baseline in the MS COCO image captioning task.
The Context Sequence Memory Network (CSMN) will enhance personalized image captioning performance compared to state-of-the-art captioning models.
Sharing knowledge with temporally-directed unsupervised video prediction and logically-directed language entailment generation will improve video captioning performance.
Generating entire paragraphs for describing images will provide detailed, unified stories that overcome limitations of existing image captioning methods.
Incorporating high-level concepts into the CNN-RNN approach achieves a significant improvement on the state-of-the-art in both image captioning and visual question answering.
The Multimodal Transformer model will significantly outperform previous state-of-the-art methods in image captioning by capturing both intra- and inter-modal interactions.
Direct Assessment method for evaluating automatic video captions will provide a more reliable measure of caption quality compared to traditional metrics like BLEU and METEOR.
The proposed method outperforms the baselines for automatic figure caption generation.
Training a grounded semantic parser using captioned videos can significantly expand the range of data that parsers can be trained on and lower the effort of training a semantic parser.
A joint visual-linguistic model will learn high-level features without any explicit supervision and outperform the state-of-the-art on video captioning.
The coherence recurrent convolutional network (CRCN) outperforms other state-of-the-art candidate methods in retrieving a sequence of natural sentences for an image stream.

Long Short-Term Memory with Attributes (LSTM-A) will improve image captioning performance by integrating attributes and inter-attribute correlations into the Convolutional Neural Networks and Recurrent Neural Networks framework.
Deep Embedded Memory Networks will outperform other question-answering models in video story question-answering tasks.
Attention models can benefit from explicit human gaze labels for the task of video captioning.
Hierarchical Attention Network (HAN) significantly improves the performance of image captioning compared to existing methods.
The heterogeneous memory enhanced multimodal attention model for video question answering will achieve state-of-the-art performance on four VideoQA benchmark datasets.
The hierarchical spatio-temporal attention network will effectively learn the joint representation of dynamic video contents for open-ended video question answering.
The model generates images with novel scene compositions corresponding to previously unseen captions in the dataset.
The multi-task triple-stream network (MTTSNet) will generate more diverse and richer representations in image captioning compared to several baselines and competing methods.
